item.title: Download the live set of Artefakt’s new track Undertow
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
In a new interview with Ableton, Nick Lapien breaks down the production process behind Artefakt’s latest release.

The post Download the live set of Artefakt’s new track <i>Undertow</i> appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
In a new interview with Ableton, Nick Lapien breaks down the production process behind Artefakt’s latest chapter.

Artefakt’s sound was originally shaped by the collision of two different musical perspectives – Robin Koek’s love of early Detroit techno and Nick Lapien’s background in ambient and experimental music. That balance between hypnotic grooves and atmospheric detail became central to the project’s identity. Now continuing solo following Koek’s departure, Lapien is carrying that foundation into a new era while gradually introducing fresh ideas and production approaches.

In a new interview with Ableton, Lapien reflects on his newest EP, which marks the beginning of this next phase for Artefakt. One of its standout cuts, Undertow, captures Lapien’s evolving approach, starting with field recordings captured on the Greek island of Agistri.

Discussing how Undertow first came together, he says: “I had a relatively quiet week there with my field recorder. I started recording things I found on hikes – branches, twigs, natural objects, things like that. There was a tree that was slightly split, and if you hit it in slightly different locations, it created a really nice resonance. Then in the evening, back at the hotel, I started messing around with the sounds and trying to bring some movement into the shorter loops I had created during the day. The initial idea was to create the track as much as possible from those recordings.”

In the interview, Lapien also emphasises the importance of a more tactile, performance-led approach to music-making, encouraging producers to move away from overly rigid workflows. “I strongly believe in being hands-on. Playing things in rather than programming everything with a mouse helps a lot,” he says. “If you’re actually playing, drumming on pads, using the Ableton Push, or a MIDI keyboard, then you really have your own timing. You’re playing chords in a certain way. Every person has a different tendency to look for certain harmonies.”

Download the live set to Artefakt’s new track Undertow, and read the full interview unpacking his production process on Ableton’s website. Listen to the track on SoundCloud.

The post Download the live set of Artefakt’s new track <i>Undertow</i> appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Duskus headlines cave rave in Peak District as part of Columbia Hike Society’s HikeFest
item.dc:creator: Loulou Ward
item.description:
Hundreds of hikers trekked across moorland to reach a hidden cave for an intimate underground set from the London-based producer.

The post Duskus headlines cave rave in Peak District as part of Columbia Hike Society’s HikeFest appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of hikers trekked across moorland to reach a hidden cave for an intimate underground set from the London-based producer.

Columbia Hike Society’s HikeFest took place over the weekend, with its attendees hiking deep into the Peak District before descending into a hidden cave rave headlined by London-based electronic producer and DJ Duskus.

Hikers were given the exact coordinates of the cave at the start of the day, before being guided by Columbia Hike Society leaders to the subterranean venue, where its natural acoustics and environmental sounds became part of the performance itself. With limited phone signal in the remote location, ravers were able to disconnect from their devices entirely. 

By blending emotional, melodic elements with 4×4 house, lo-fi and UK garage, Duskus draws inspiration from the natural world, making his sound a natural fit for a gig that was as much about the journey as the music. 

Columbia Hike Society is hosting over 30 free guided hikes across the UK this summer. Get tickets here. 

The post Duskus headlines cave rave in Peak District as part of Columbia Hike Society’s HikeFest appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: We Love Green completes line-up for 15th anniversary edition
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival returns to Paris from 5–7 June with Addison Rae, Little Simz, Oklou, Jim Legxacy and more set to perform.

The post We Love Green completes line-up for 15th anniversary edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival returns to Paris from 5–7 June with Addison Rae, Little Simz, Oklou and Jim Legxacy set to perform.

We Love Green has completed the line-up for its 15th anniversary edition, taking place from 5–7 June at Bois de Vincennes. Across three days, the Paris festival will combine live music, talks and cultural activations.

Friday, 5 June will be headlined by Gorillaz, with performances from Little Simz, Dijon, Jim Legxacy, DJ Gigola, Qendresa and more.

On Saturday, 6 June, Theodora and Addison Rae top the bill, alongside sets from Oklou, KI/KI, Overmono, Loukeman and Alewya.

Closing the festival on Sunday, 7 June are headliners The xx and Charlotte Cardin. The final day will also feature performances from Ethel Cain, BB Trickz, Ninajirachi and a b2b set from Bambounou and HAAi, among others.

Known for its environmentally conscious approach, We Love Green continues to position itself as one of Europe’s leading sustainable music festivals, with a fully vegetarian food offering and eco-designed production across the site.

Passes are on sale now via We Love Green. Get your Eurostar tickets for the festival here.

The post We Love Green completes line-up for 15th anniversary edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Le Guess Who? announces 24-hour dub programme
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The Bug, Scientist, Introspekt, Tash LC and more will perform during a nonstop 24-hour dub session in Utrecht this November.

The post Le Guess Who? announces 24-hour dub programme appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Bug, Scientist, Introspekt, Tash LC and more will perform during a nonstop 24-hour dub session in Utrecht this November.

Le Guess Who? has revealed details for 24 HOUR DUB, a new continuous 24-hour programme taking place during the festival’s 20th anniversary edition in Utrecht from 7–8 November.

Held at KABUL à GoGo, the event will run from 4pm on Saturday through to 4pm on Sunday, bringing together artists, DJs, poets and producers connected to dub music and bass culture from around the world.

The line-up includes Aba Shanti-I, Mutabaruka, CARISTA, Tash LC, Introspekt, Greentea Peng performing as Greentea Selecta, The Bug feat. Warrior Queen, The Bug vs Ghost Dubs, and more.

Scientist will perform material from his 1981 album Scientist Rids the World of the Evil Curse of The Vampires, while Mad Professor is set to live mix trance-inducing polyrhythms from Nakibembe Embaire Group, and also appear with Sista Aisha.

The event is being presented in collaboration with PRESSURE by The Bug and Touching Bass. Krackfree Soundsystem will provide a custom-built sound system for the weekend.

Dedicated passes will be available for the full 24-hour programme, alongside separate night and morning entry options. Find out more here. Tickets go on sale on 2 June via Le Guess Who?.

The post Le Guess Who? announces 24-hour dub programme appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: MUTEK Montréal finalises this year’s music programme
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival returns to Montréal from 25-30 August with sets from Kali Malone, Facta, gyrofield, Ben UFO and more.

The post MUTEK Montréal finalises this year’s music programme appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival returns to Montréal from 25-30 August with sets from Facta, gyrofield, Ben UFO and more.

MUTEK Montréal has unveiled the final wave of artists set to appear at its 2026 edition, which takes place across the Canadian city from 25-30 August.

New additions include Kali Malone, Kara-Lis Coverdale, Debit, Julian Sartorius, Zora Jones, Ah! Kosmos and Hainbach, and Poirier.

Spread across six days, the festival brings together live electronic music, audiovisual performance and digital art installations through a programme spanning both ticketed venues and free public events.

This year’s edition will take place across spaces including the Society for Arts and Technology, MTELUS and Maison symphonique, while Les Grands Ballets’ Studio-Theatre joins the festival for the first time. The venue will host the returning Play series, which provides audiovisual artists with a dedicated space for image-making and performance.

Among the returning strands is the Nocturne series at SAT, which will host late-night performances from artists including Barker, Purelink with Mika Oki, gyrofield and Mia Koden.

The X/Visions series heads to Maison symphonique for three nights centred on contemporary composition and experimental electronics, featuring Voices From The Lake and Kali Malone. Meanwhile, MTELUS hosts two all-night Métropolis sessions with names including Ben UFO, Facta, Jeff Mills and Polygonia.

The Esplanade Tranquille will continue to host the open-air strand, with nightly performances beginning at 5pm.

The 2026 programme has been curated with each venue’s atmosphere and architecture in mind.

All ticket options are now on sale here.

The post MUTEK Montréal finalises this year’s music programme appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: HARD Summer Festival announces additions to 2026 line-up
item.dc:creator: Loulou Ward
item.description:
The LA festival returns to Hollywood Park from 1-2 August.

The post HARD Summer Festival announces additions to 2026 line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The LA festival returns to Hollywood Park from 1-2 August.

HARD Summer has announced a fresh round of line-up additions for its 2026 edition, taking place at Hollywood Park in Inglewood, California, this August. 

The newly announced include Japanese experimentalist ¥ØU$UK€ ¥UK1MAT$U, DJ and production duo RØZ, an exciting Brutalismus 3000 b2b Boyz Noise pairing and alternative dance project 1tbsp. 

They join the previously announced acts, including Kali Uchis, Confidence Man, VTSS, Shygirl Presents Club Shy and many more.

This year’s festival will see a Purple Stage takeover on 2 August from Berlin-based artist collective and record label Live From Earth. Feature 10cust, 2hollis, Frost Children, MCR-T, Snow Strippers and Underscores, a notably more underground offering than the festival’s main stages. 

Look back at last year’s edition here.

Passes are on sale now.

The post HARD Summer Festival announces additions to 2026 line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: BERSHKA MUSIC and DICE announce 2026 collaboration
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The fourth consecutive year of the partnership will see expanded presence across global music scenes, from Europe to Brazil.

The post BERSHKA MUSIC and DICE announce 2026 collaboration appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The fourth consecutive year of the partnership will see expanded presence across global music scenes, from Europe to Brazil.

BERSHKA MUSIC and ticketing platform DICE have announced the continuation of their partnership into 2026, marking four years of collaboration aimed at supporting and amplifying live music culture worldwide.

The ongoing project will see BERSHKA MUSIC deepen its presence across concerts, festivals and club nights throughout Europe, the UK, Miami and Brazil.

DICE will once again play a key role in keeping BERSHKA MUSIC closely connected to the live music ecosystem, working alongside leading promoters and culturally influential events. 

Through its BERSHKA MUSIC initiative, the brand will reconnect with established partners such as Nalgas Club in Barcelona, Antídoto Club in Madrid and RALLY Festival in London. At the same time, the 2026 program will introduce new collaborators, including The Rocky Desert in Ibiza and Tijolo Records in Brazil.

Fans can engage with the partnership directly via DICE, where following BERSHKA MUSIC will provide first access to event announcements, as well as curated content from the events and artists shaping culture right now, opportunities to win tickets to new events every month, and the chance to win DICE credit. Follow here.

The post BERSHKA MUSIC and DICE announce 2026 collaboration appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: ASIAN DOPE BOYS, Ziúr and more to play at Kuboraum’s Venice Biennale takeover
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Kuboraum will present ‘We Travel to Know Our Own Geography’ during the Venice Art Biennale this weekend.

The post ASIAN DOPE BOYS, Ziúr and more to play at Kuboraum’s Venice Biennale takeover appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Kuboraum will present another chapter of their We Travel to Know Our Own Geography during the Venice Art Biennale this weekend.

Kuboraum unveils a four-day program under the umbrella of their We Travel to Know Our Own Geography series from 6–9 May in Venice alongside the opening of the 61st Venice Art Biennale.

Taking place at the Pier Fortunato Calvi State Secondary School in Castello 1808, the line-up spans performance art, music, multidisciplinary works and special collaborations.

Marking the upcoming joint release of Ocean Cage by KADAPAT & Nova Ruth featuring Siko Setyanto – issued via Kuboraum Editions and SVBKVLT – ASIAN DOPE BOYS director Tianzhuo Chen will team up with Siko Setyanto to premiere a stage-based interpretation of the record, Moyang 先祖 & Seaman 漁師, presented over two days.

Other collaborations include Joshua Serafin performing Relics: An Eye Once Blind with Görkem Şen and Pierre Bayet, Kianí del Valle teaming up with Ziúr to present De Brujas y Fantasma, FRANKIE and Kelman Duran presenting McArthur, and Ziúr & Sandi’s performance of Home with Martina Bertoni and Sara Persico.

Additional shows come from Emiliano Maggi, Romain Azzaro, and Cosimo Damiano.

Previously, Kuboraum has presented We Travel to Know Our Own Geography alongside other events such as Terraforma Festival, Dover Street Market Paris, and Hamburger Bahnhof in Berlin.

The post ASIAN DOPE BOYS, Ziúr and more to play at Kuboraum’s Venice Biennale takeover appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: La Nature reveals curators for 2026 edition
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The Belgian Ardennes festival returns in June with a collective-led programme.

The post La Nature reveals curators for 2026 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Belgian Ardennes festival returns in June with a collective-led programme.

La Nature Festival will return to the forests of Baraque de Fraiture, near Vielsalm in the Belgian Ardennes, from 18 to 22 June 2026, marking its seventh edition and 16 years of the NoName collective behind it.

While the full line-up will not be revealed in advance, organisers have revealed a roster of programming curators drawn from across Europe’s electronic scenes.

The 2026 edition will be shaped by NoName alongside guests including Turin-based sound artist Katatonic Silentio and Liège-based multidisciplinary artist Simon Médard, alongside Paris and Berlin-based music and art collective Crépite, Daniel Danieli’s Whispering Signals label, Brussels-based dance label Maloca, and Brussels-based DJ collective and label RebelUp. Each is set to develop distinct sonic and spatial identities across the site.

Programming also extends into performance, installation and participatory practices. Daytime activities curated by the collective Moojo will emphasise wellbeing and collective experience, including workshops, ambient sessions and body-focused rituals.

In addition to music, La Nature will host multidisciplinary works selected for their ability to integrate with the forest setting. Some installations may remain accessible after the festival, forming a longer-term public art trail in collaboration with local partners.

This year, the festival is moving away from assigning an annual theme. Instead, it will follow a longer-term artistic manifesto focused on immersion, minimal intervention and a close relationship with the natural environment.

Read La Nature’s full manifesto at the festival’s website.

The post La Nature reveals curators for 2026 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Solstice Festival expands 2026 line-up
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The Finnish festival adds a new stage curated with NYC-based community station The Lot Radio to this year's programme.

The post Solstice Festival expands 2026 line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Finnish festival adds a new stage curated with NYC-based community station The Lot Radio

Solstice Festival has released the complete line-up for its 2026 edition, which will take place from 18 to 20 June at Rukatunturi – a 500-metre-high fell in northern Finland.

A key addition is The Hut, a 150-capacity dance floor positioned on a plateau overlooking the forests and lakes surrounding Ruka, which will focus on experimental club sounds. Its programming is curated in collaboration with The Lot Radio, which marks its tenth anniversary in 2026.

Artists announced for the stage include Kiernan Laveaux, livwutang and DJ Fart In The Club, alongside The Lot Radio’s own deep creep. Additional names on the bill include RHR, Verraco, LYZZA, Toma Kami, mad miran b2b Loidis, Desto and Mary Young.

They join a previously announced roster featuring Sonja Moonear, DJ Masda, CEM, DJ Koolt and KMRU (live), as well as the Mad Professor Live Dub Show, Trickpony, Xiaolin, and Sansibar & Spekki Webu, among others.

Further details about the after-hours programme will be shared later in the spring, with late-night events scheduled to take place in Ruka Village following each festival day.

Tickets are available here.

The post Solstice Festival expands 2026 line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Listen to Boards of Canada’s first new music in 13 years
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
After mounting speculation about the duo’s return, new track 'Tape 05' has just been uploaded to Boards of Canada’s YouTube channel.

The post Listen to Boards of Canada’s first new music in 13 years appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
New track Tape 05 has just been uploaded to Boards of Canada’s YouTube channel.

After 13 years, Boards of Canada are officially back with new song Tape 05, uploaded to YouTube today (16 April).

The release arrives amid mounting speculation about the duo’s comeback, sparked by a series of mysterious VHS tapes that began appearing at random homes earlier this month. The unmarked cassette – reportedly distributed via Ochre, a music logistics platform closely associated with Warp Records – featured degraded analogue visuals, shortwave-style audio, and fragmented, layered voices. Aside from cryptic hexagon graphics, the tapes carried no branding.

Posters featuring the Hexagon Sun symbol began appearing in London and LA last week, followed by a cryptic billboard campaign led by Warp yesterday.

Boards of Canada’s last official full-length studio album is Tomorrow’s Harvest, released in June 2013.

Listen to the new release below.

The post Listen to Boards of Canada’s first new music in 13 years appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Massive Attack and Tom Waits release ‘Boots On The Ground’
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The protest song, titled 'Boots On The Ground', is accompanied by a seven-minute video by the photographer thefinaleye.

The post Massive Attack and Tom Waits release ‘Boots On The Ground’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Massive Attack and Tom Waits have joined forces to release Boots On The Ground, alongside a video examining political unrest and systemic injustice in the United States.

The accompanying film, which runs for seven minutes, was created in collaboration with the documentary photographer known as thefinaleye.

“The film that follows encapsulates his work across an epoch forming six-year period,” reads the intro. “From the murder of George Floyd in Minneapolis on May 5 2020, to recent ICE raids on migrant communities & the killing of civilians that protect them. From the brutal state repression of public protest, to the reality of American homelessness that includes nearly 33,000 military veterans.”

The video closes by memorialising those who have died in connection with immigration enforcement and highlighting the broader consequences of state retaliation against protest movements.

In a statement accompanying the release, Massive Attack frame the song as a response to a broader political climate. “Across the western hemisphere, state authoritarianism and the militarisation of police forces are fusing again with neo-fascist politics. Seen within the American emergency, at home and overseas, this track contains pulses of callous impulse and abandoned mind,” they said.

“One day many years ago I accepted an invitation from Massive Attack to collaborate,” Waits explains. “Way back then, we sent them Boots on the Ground. Their long release delay never worried me. Today, as in all of mankind’s yesterdays, guarantees this song will never go out of style.”

For Waits, it is his first new material since his 2011 album Bad As Me, with additional vocals contributed by his son Casey.

The track arrives days after Massive Attack frontman Robert Del Naja was among hundreds arrested at a recent Palestine Action protest.

Watch the video below.

The post Massive Attack and Tom Waits release ‘Boots On The Ground’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Live Nation operated as illegal monopoly, jury finds
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The verdict follows a seven-week New York trial and four days of deliberations, with penalties still to be determined

The post Live Nation operated as illegal monopoly, jury finds appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The verdict follows a seven-week New York trial and four days of deliberations, with penalties still to be determined.

A federal jury has concluded that Live Nation, the parent company of Ticketmaster, unlawfully maintained a monopoly in the live entertainment market. After four days of deliberations, jurors determined that the company has violated federal and state antitrust laws.

While the ruling establishes liability, the consequences for Live Nation have yet to be finalised. Potential penalties could include substantial financial damages or structural changes to the company, including a possible split from Ticketmaster. Live Nation acquired the ticketing platform in 2009.

Jurors found that Ticketmaster had overcharged customers by $1.72 (£1.27) per ticket over several years, a figure that is expected to serve as a baseline for calculating total damages.

The case stems from a lawsuit filed in May 2024 by the U.S. Department of Justice, which argued that the company’s practices suppressed competition, resulting in higher prices and reduced service quality. Although the Justice Department later reached a preliminary settlement with Live Nation – one which did not mandate a breakup of Ticketmaster and Live Nation, something it had initially called for – and withdrew from the trial, more than 30 states continued to pursue the case.

Public and political scrutiny intensified after high-profile ticketing issues during major tours, including widespread complaints tied to Taylor Swift’s 2022 Eras Tour.

“The jury’s verdict is not the last word on this matter,” Live Nation said in a statement following the April 15 decision. “Pending motions will determine whether the liability and damages rulings stand.”

The post Live Nation operated as illegal monopoly, jury finds appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Gaswerk Music Days sets 2026 dates with first artist announcement
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
MC Yallah & Scotch Rolex, Holy Tongue and others lead the initial line-up for the Berlin festival spread across two weekends.

The post Gaswerk Music Days sets 2026 dates with first artist announcement appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
MC Yallah & Scotch Rolex, Holy Tongue and others lead the initial line-up for the Berlin festival spread across two weekends.

Gaswerk Music Days has shared its first artist announcement for 2026, outlining plans for the next edition.

Among the acts confirmed so far are MC Yallah and Scotch Rolex, Holy Tongue (Valentina Magaletti and Al Wootton), and Burnt Friedman with João Pais Filipe. Maurice Louca will present Barĩy, joined by Jay Mitta, while Ancestral Futures brings together Ibaaku and K’boko. Azu Tiwaline, Rabih Beaini and Embryo are also on the bill.

The festival is scheduled across two weekends – 24–26 July and 31 July–2 August – at Gaswerksiedlung, a former industrial housing complex now used as studio space for hundreds of artists.

Alongside the performances, the project includes a residency programme linking Berlin-based artists with collaborators from across the African continent, developed with Nyege Nyege and other independent partners. The residencies focus on research and production, with work often carried through to the live programme.

Gaswerk’s activities extend beyond the summer event through a year-round series of concerts and formats, including Modula and Crazy Mega Jam. Upcoming dates tied to the programme include Modula V on 23 April at Gaswerksiedlung, Das Ende der Liebe with Mononoke and Shiva & the Destroyer on 3 July, and KUF with UnoX at Kantine am Berghain on 10 July.

Further artists and programme details will be announced soon.

The post Gaswerk Music Days sets 2026 dates with first artist announcement appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Madonna shares first track teaser for ‘Confessions On A Dance Floor: Part II’
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Listen to the preview of 'I Feel So Free', the first taste of new music from 'Confessions On A Dance Floor: Part II'.

The post Madonna shares first track teaser for ‘Confessions On A Dance Floor: Part II’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Listen to the preview of the new track, I Feel So Free.

Madonna has officially announced Confessions On A Dance Floor: Part II, confirming long-standing speculation about a follow-up to her 2005 album. The new record is set for release on 3 July 2026 and will mark her first album in seven years.

The artist has hinted at returning to one of her most celebrated eras since sharing studio footage featuring original Confessions producer Stuart Price in December 2024.

Excitement intensified yesterday (14 April) when Madonna wiped her Instagram account, before returning with an official announcement and artwork captioned: Confessions On A Dance Floor: Part II — July 3 2026, and adding the new project to her website.

Listen to the snippet of I Feel So Free below.

The post Madonna shares first track teaser for ‘Confessions On A Dance Floor: Part II’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Italy’s Limbo festival completes line-up
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Daniele Baldelli and Napoli Segreta among the programme highlights as the festival returns to Tuscany this July.

The post Italy’s Limbo festival completes line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Daniele Baldelli and Napoli Segreta among the programme highlights as the festival returns to Tuscany this July.

Limbo Festival returns to Tuscany from 10–12 July to “Il Ciocco – The Living Mountain” in Barga, Lucca, in the green Garfagnana area of Tuscany, with a line-up that blends international names and Italian talent.

Among the standouts are Daniele Baldelli, a key figure in Balearic history, and the Neapolitan duo Napoli Segreta.

They’re joined by a wide-ranging cast including Stavroz (live) and Oceanvs Orientalis (live), alongside Italian selectors and live acts such as Clap! Clap!, Luca Bacchetti and DRĖĖĖMY. Across the weekend, the programme moves between DJ sets, performances and hybrid shows.

Beyond the music, the festival will offer a full schedule of wellness sessions, rituals, sunset ceremonies, mindfulness workshops and talks, organised under this year’s manifesto: The New Era, asking what it means to be human today.

The programme also expands into cultural conversations with contributions from The Arcana Society, La Era Nuova, Giosè Milli (Istituto Hoffman), Sandro Voi, Lucca Biennale Cartasiam and more, focusing on creativity and wellbeing.

Food and nature also remain central to the experience, with the site’s 600-hectare natural estate site featuring a resort, two restaurants, a sports village and 42 kilometres of trails and cycling paths.

Get tickets here.

The post Italy’s Limbo festival completes line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Wireless Festival cancelled
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The news comes after this year's headliner, Kanye West, was banned from entering the UK by the government today.

The post Wireless Festival cancelled appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The news comes after this year’s headliner, Kanye West, was banned from entering the UK by the government.

“As a result of the Home Office banning YE from entering the United Kingdom, Wireless Festival has been forced to cancel,” a statement on Instagram reads. “All ticket holders will receive an automatic refund.”

Earlier today, the Home Office confirmed that West had applied to travel to the UK via an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA), but ministers acted after mounting pressure for both his appearance to be cancelled and for a wider ban on his entry. It was then confirmed that the artist will not be allowed into the UK.

The decision came a week after the festival line-up was revealed. West had been scheduled to headline all three nights of the event, taking place from 10–12 July.

Wireless Festival’s decision to book the rapper as its headline act sparked firm backlash, with senior political figures including Prime Minister Keir Starmer and London Mayor Sadiq Khan among those criticising the move. Concern centres on his long history of antisemitic, pro-Nazi and racist remarks, and the message sent by continuing to platform him.

Prime Minister Keir Starmer said it was “deeply concerning” that West had been booked “despite his previous antisemitic remarks and celebration of Nazism”.

Pressure on organisers escalated over the weekend, with several major sponsors distancing themselves from the event. Pepsi pulled out of its sponsorship role, followed by Rockstar Energy, while payment partner PayPal will no longer appear in upcoming promotional campaigns.

Michael Weiger, chief executive of the Board of Deputies of British Jews, was among those calling for the UK to refuse West entry. The organisation also accused Wireless Festival, its parent company Festival Republic and managing director Melvin Benn of “profiteering from racism”.

“We think that would be a very appropriate step were the home secretary to find a way to not allow him into the country,” he said.

Earlier this week, West said he “would be grateful” for the opportunity to meet members of the UK’s Jewish community in light of the controversy.

In response, the Board of Deputies said it would be willing to meet the rapper, but only if he withdraws from his planned Wireless performances.

“The Jewish community will want to see a genuine remorse and change before believing that the appropriate place to test this sincerity is on the main stage at the Wireless Festival,” said the Board’s president, Phil Rosenberg.

The artist has already faced travel restrictions elsewhere, with Australia cancelling his visa after the release of Heil Hitler last May.

The post Wireless Festival cancelled appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Kanye West blocked from entering the UK
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The government has denied Kanye West permission to enter the country following controversy surrounding his planned Wireless appearance.

The post Kanye West blocked from entering the UK appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The UK government has denied Kanye West permission to enter the country following controversy surrounding his planned headline performance at this summer’s Wireless Festival.

The Home Office confirmed that West had applied to travel to the UK via an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA), but ministers acted after mounting pressure for both his appearance to be cancelled and for a wider ban on his entry.

It has now been confirmed that the artist, who now goes by Ye, will not be allowed into the UK.

The decision comes a week after the festival line-up was revealed. Wireless promoted the announcement with an Instagram video of West performing, which also mentioned Pepsi UK’s account. He had been scheduled to headline all three nights of the event, taking place from 10–12 July.

Wireless Festival’s decision to book the rapper as its headline act sparked firm backlash, with senior political figures including Prime Minister Keir Starmer and London Mayor Sadiq Khan among those criticising the move. Concern centres on his long history of antisemitic, pro-Nazi and racist remarks, and the message sent by continuing to platform him.

Prime Minister Keir Starmer said it was “deeply concerning” that West had been booked “despite his previous antisemitic remarks and celebration of Nazism”.

Pressure on organisers escalated over the weekend, with several major sponsors distancing themselves from the event. Pepsi pulled out of its sponsorship role, followed by Rockstar Energy, while payment partner PayPal will no longer appear in upcoming promotional campaigns.

Michael Weiger, chief executive of the Board of Deputies of British Jews, was among those calling for the UK to refuse West entry. The organisation also accused Wireless Festival, its parent company Festival Republic and managing director Melvin Benn of “profiteering from racism”.

“We think that would be a very appropriate step were the home secretary to find a way to not allow him into the country,” he said.

Earlier this week, West said he “would be grateful” for the opportunity to meet members of the UK’s Jewish community in light of the controversy.

In response, the Board of Deputies said it would be willing to meet the rapper, but only if he withdraws from his planned Wireless performances.

“The Jewish community will want to see a genuine remorse and change before believing that the appropriate place to test this sincerity is on the main stage at the Wireless Festival,” said the Board’s president, Phil Rosenberg.

The artist has already faced travel restrictions elsewhere, with Australia cancelling his visa after the release of Heil Hitler last May.

Wireless is yet to comment.

The post Kanye West blocked from entering the UK appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Open’er Festival expands 2026 bill with new wave of artists
item.dc:creator: Julia Bottoms
item.description:
Jehnny Beth, Bassvictim and more join the line-up for the Polish festival, which returns to Gdynia from 1-4 July.

The post Open’er Festival expands 2026 bill with new wave of artists appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Jehnny Beth, Bassvictim and more join the line-up for the Polish festival. 

Returning to Gdynia at the Gdynia–Kosakowo Airport grounds on Poland’s Baltic coast from 1-4 July, Open’er will mark its 23rd year this summer. 

Among the latest additions, Florence + The Machine will take to the Orange Main Stage on 1 July, with London dance duo Joy (Anonymous) confirmed for the same day on the Alter Stage. 

On July 3, Panda Bear & Sonic Boom will play the Alter Stage. Former Crack Magazine cover star Jehnny Beth and London duo Bassvictim also join the bill.

They join other previously confirmed artists, former Crack Magazine cover stars IDLES, David Byrne, PinkPatheress, and Ethel Cain, as well as The Cure, Nick Cave and the Bad Seeds, Addison Rae, Kneecap and Jade.

Look back on last year’s edition through five key performances and get tickets here.

 

The post Open’er Festival expands 2026 bill with new wave of artists appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Thea Gajić’s ‘SURVIVING EARTH’ will be released in UK & Ireland cinemas this month
item.dc:creator: Julia Bottoms
item.description:
Thea Gajić’s feature debut arrives in cinemas on 24 April, following premieres at SXSW and the Edinburgh International Film Festival.

The post Thea Gajić’s ‘SURVIVING EARTH’ will be released in UK & Ireland cinemas this month appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Writer and director Thea Gajić’s feature debut SURVIVING EARTH will open in UK and Ireland cinemas on April 24 2026, following premieres at SXSW and the Edinburgh International Film Festival.

Based on a true story, Surviving Earth follows Vlad (Slavko Sobin), a gifted harmonica player who arrived in the UK in the 1990s after fleeing the conflict in Yugoslavia. Now settled in Bristol and working as a counsellor, he reconnects with his roots and passion for Balkan music by forming a band with colleagues from work.

As the group chases success, however, Vlad finds himself haunted by the trauma of his past as the life he has carefully rebuilt begins to unravel, with his beloved only daughter, Maria (Olive Gray), the hardest hit. 

The cast also includes Stuart Martin, Peter Coonan, Toni Gojanović and Ann Ogbomo. 

Gajić, who was born and raised in South London, previously won the New Talent Award at BFI Future Film Festival for her short RUN and was selected for the Sundance Ignite Fellowship.

Gajić said of the project: “Making this film has changed the way I look at myself and the people around me. It has allowed me to extend grace further than I thought possible, and I only hope it does the same for others. 

“I am so excited for our release in the spring and for audiences to finally be a part of its story and further conversation.”

Watch the trailer below.

The post Thea Gajić’s ‘SURVIVING EARTH’ will be released in UK & Ireland cinemas this month appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Watch underscores play SOPHIE, ML Buch, Kylie Minogue and more inside a Koreatown dry cleaners
item.dc:creator: Julia Bottoms
item.description:
The artists plays SOPHIE, ML Buch, Oklou, FKA twigs, Kylie Minogue and more in a new live mix for the platform goyo club.

The post Watch underscores play SOPHIE, ML Buch, Kylie Minogue and more inside a Koreatown dry cleaners appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The artist cleans up in a new hyperpop set for goyo club.

In a recorded set for the platform goyo club, underscores delivers an hour-long mix that fuses internet-era hyperpop chaos with pop bangers – think SOPHIE, Ninajirachi, Jane Remover and Oklou, alongside classics from Janet Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Skrillex, Rihanna and Miley Cyrus.

The artist is the latest to feature in goyo club’s DJ mix series, eclectic cleaners, filmed each inside a real, family-owned dry cleaners in LA’s Koreatown.

The series is dedicated to giving local and visiting artists a stage in family-owned businesses across the city, spaces that “carry history, memory and community”.

It’s an apt setting for an artist whose works reveal a long-standing fascination with the romance between liminal, commercial third spaces and immaterial, dream-like ones. 

Drawing from her recently released third album U, Innuendo (I Get U) flows into Nick León & Erika de Casier’s Bikini, while Tell Me (U Want It) collides with FKA twigs’ Drums of Death and Gwen Stefani’s Hollaback Girl.

Watch the full set below. 

The post Watch underscores play SOPHIE, ML Buch, Kylie Minogue and more inside a Koreatown dry cleaners appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Art Fund study suggests looking at art might be good for you
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Research indicates gallery visits can quickly lower stress while engaging the body and mind.

The post Art Fund study suggests looking at art might be good for you appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Research indicates gallery visits can lower stress while engaging the body and mind.

A new study by King’s College London, working with Art Fund and the Psychiatry Research Trust, suggests that viewing original artworks can have an immediate effect on physical wellbeing.

The research followed 50 volunteers aged 18 to 40, comparing those who viewed well-known paintings in a London gallery with others who saw reproductions in a similar but non-gallery setting. The results indicate that seeing art in person can influence several systems in the body at once, including those linked to stress and hormones.

One of the clearest findings was a drop in cortisol, a hormone associated with stress. Those in the gallery group saw levels fall by an average of 22 percent, while the reproduction group experienced a smaller decrease of 8 percent.

Markers linked to inflammation also declined among participants who viewed original artworks, suggesting a potential calming effect on the body. Meanwhile, changes in heart rate and skin temperature pointed to increased engagement and emotional response.

The project also found that art activates the endocrine, immune and autonomic nervous systems all at once – something that has never been previously recorded.

“From a scientific perspective, the most exciting outtake is that art had a positive impact on three different body systems – the immune, endocrine and autonomic systems – at the same time,” Dr Tony Woods, researcher at Kings College London, said. “This is a unique finding and something we were genuinely surprised to see.”

“Stress hormones and inflammatory markers like cortisol, IL-6 and TNF-alpha are linked to a wide range of health problems, from heart disease and diabetes to anxiety and depression. The fact that viewing original art lowered these markers suggests that cultural experiences may play a real role in protecting both mind and body.”

The study took place between July and September 2025 at The Courtauld Gallery in London. Participants viewed works by artists such as Van Gogh and Gauguin, while researchers monitored physiological responses using wearable devices and saliva samples.

“This study proves for the first time what we’ve long felt at Art Fund – that art really is good for you. What’s particularly exciting is that the findings show these benefits are universal – they can be experienced by anyone,” Art Fund Director, Jenny Waldman, said. “We want to encourage everyone to make time to visit their local museum or gallery and experience these powerful effects for themselves. With a National Art Pass, you can enjoy free or discounted entry to hundreds of inspiring places across the UK – and discover just how good art can make you feel.”

Find the data here.

The post Art Fund study suggests looking at art might be good for you appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: MOMO Festival returns to Rotterdam next month
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The multidisciplinary event spans music, performance and art, with Obongjayar, Wesley Joseph and Mandy, Indiana on the bill.

The post MOMO Festival returns to Rotterdam next month appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The multidisciplinary event spans music, performance and art, with Obongjayar, Wesley Joseph and Mandy, Indiana on the bill.

MOMO Festival is back in Rotterdam from 16–18 April this year, taking over venues across the city.

This year’s line-up includes Ivor Novello-winning artist Obongjayar, alongside Wesley Joseph, who releases his new album Forever Ends Someday in April, and Manchester group Mandy, Indiana. They are joined by German indie-electropop project Zimmer90, Swedish band Boko Yout, spoken word artist and musician Joshua Idehen, Hackney-born singer Bel Cobain, Amsterdam duo Weval, and South London artist BINA.

The festival’s residency programme also returns for its fourth edition, this time featuring Amsterdam-based band Nusantara Beat. Known for blending traditional Indonesian sounds with vintage pop influences, the six-piece will present a series of performances across the weekend, building on their previous appearance at the festival in 2023.

Get your tickets now here.

The post MOMO Festival returns to Rotterdam next month appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Paléo Festival Nyon’s 2026 line-up is led by Lorde and The Cure
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The Swiss festival has unveiled the programme for its 49th edition, which is set to take place from 21 to 26 July.

The post Paléo Festival Nyon’s 2026 line-up is led by Lorde and The Cure appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival has unveiled the programme for its 49th edition, set to take place in July.

Paléo Festival Nyon will return to western Switzerland from 21 to 26 July, with Lorde, The Cure and Gorillaz among the top-billed acts. The line-up spans pop, rock, hip-hop and electronic music, with artists including The Last Dinner Party and Amelie Lens also confirmed.

Performances will take place across eight stages, including the Grande Scène and Véga for headline shows, alongside areas dedicated to electronic music, global programming and new talent. More than 200 concerts and performances are planned across the six days.

A central focus this year is the Village du Monde, which highlights Nordic countries – Finland, Norway, Sweden, Iceland and Denmark, alongside the Faroe Islands and Greenland. Around 15 artists will feature on the programme, including Norwegian black metal group Witch Club Satan.

Swiss talent will also be strongly represented, with 16 acts on the bill.

Tickets go on sale on 25 March at midday via the festival’s official website. The online waiting room opens at 11:45 a.m. CET.

Revisit our review of last year’s festival here.

Image: © Lionel Flusin

The post Paléo Festival Nyon’s 2026 line-up is led by Lorde and The Cure appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: MUTEK Montréal announces first artists for 2026
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Ben UFO, Polygonia, gyrofield and more are confirmed for the Montréal festival's 27th edition this August.

The post MUTEK Montréal announces first artists for 2026 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Ben UFO, Polygonia, gyrofield and more are confirmed for the Canadian festival’s 27th edition this August.

MUTEK Montréal has revealed the first group of artists set to perform at its this year’s edition, taking place from 25–30 August.

The festival returns with six days and nights of electronic music, digital creativity and audiovisual experimentation across Montréal’s Quartier des spectacles.

The initial line-up mixes influential contemporary artists and emerging experimental voices from around the world. A second announcement focusing on Québec and Canadian talent will follow.

This year’s programme will unfold across several venues in the city’s arts district, including free outdoor programming at Esplanade Tranquille, immersive performances at the Society for Arts and Technology (SAT) and late-night dancefloor events at MTELUS. The festival will also introduce a new venue: the Maison symphonique, whose concert hall will host several large-scale works.

Among the highlights, Jeff Mills will present the North American premiere of Stargate, an immersive performance inspired by science-fiction themes. Austrian guitarist and composer Fennesz will celebrate the 25th anniversary of his landmark album Endless Summer alongside visual artist Lillevan, and A Guy Called Gerald will perform his influential 1995 album Black Secret Technology.

Other confirmed acts include Rival Consoles, Ben UFO, Barker, Mia Koden and Purelink, along with live shows from JakoJako and Polygonia.

A number of projects in this edition unfold as contemporary ritual-like performances, including Japanese collective ELECTRONICOS FANTASTICOS!, who will transform discarded electronics into musical instruments for a piece that blends sound-art, creative recycling and technological critique.

Experimental sound artist Evicshen will also present a physical performance using DIY devices and unconventional sound sources, and Québec composer Florence-Delphine Roux will debut a new project developed with the AVATAR centre.

Large-scale immersive works will take place at the Maison symphonique, including Infinity Gradient, a collaboration between composer Tristan Perich and organist James McVinnie using 100 speakers. Voices From The Lake will also make an anticipated return to the festival.

Audiovisual projects remain a central focus of the event, with new performances from Swiss composer Noémi Büchi and a cinematic audiovisual live show from Rival Consoles.

Alongside the performances, the MUTEK Forum will run from 26–28 August. This year, the programme expands across multiple cultural venues and will include talks, workshops, presentations and networking opportunities for artists, curators and digital creators. The MUTEK Market will also return, bringing together international industry professionals and supporting Canadian artists.

“The Maison symphonique opens up a new dimension for MUTEK: a monumental listening space where works take on an almost cinematic scale,” said executive co-director and artistic director Alain Mongeau. He added that the 2026 edition places greater emphasis on live performance and a walkable festival experience in the centre of the city.

Additional artists and the full MUTEK Forum programme will be announced in the coming months.

Tickets are available now.

The post MUTEK Montréal announces first artists for 2026 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Surf Festival expands debut line-up with OMOLOKO, Chaos In The CBD and more
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
OMOLOKO, Chaos In The CBD, Bradley Zero and Flo Dill are among the latest additions to the new Croatian festival.

The post Surf Festival expands debut line-up with OMOLOKO, Chaos In The CBD and more appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Bradley Zero, Leon Vynehall and Flo Dill are among the latest additions to the Croatian island gathering ahead of its first edition this June.

Set against the idyllic backdrop of Punat on the island of Krk, Croatia, Surf Festival will launch its first edition from 25 to 28 June 2026.

Focusing on both DJ sets and live performances, the programme brings together talent from across the worlds of experimental music, club culture, and radio.

Following early announcements including Livwutang, James Massiah and DJ Python, the festival has now unveiled a fresh wave of additions including Brazilian party starter OMOLOKO, Jumbi co-founder Bradley Zero, musician and producer Leon Vynehall, NTS radio host Flo Dill and New Zealand duo Chaos In The CBD.

Habibi Funk, Adriatic Social Club, Charlie Bones, CHANTSSSS, GЯEG, Rozaly, ninafterdark and more also join the line-up.

As the third wave of artists begins to take shape, organisers have also teased outdoor adventures across Krk’s pristine surroundings, alongside an extensive boat party programme set to be revealed soon.

Tickets are available now via the CoreEvent platform.

The post Surf Festival expands debut line-up with OMOLOKO, Chaos In The CBD and more appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: What to see at Somerset House Studios’ Assembly this month
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The three-day programme features felicita, Laurel Halo with Hanne Lippard, and Mark Fell with Mohammad Reza Mortazavi.

The post What to see at Somerset House Studios’ Assembly this month appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The three-day programme features felicita, Laurel Halo with Hanne Lippard, and Mark Fell with Mohammad Reza Mortazavi.

Assembly, the biannual experimental sound and music series at Somerset House Studios, returns to London from 26–28 March with a programme of performances, premieres, installations and talks.

Launched in 2018, the experimental series centres on sonic practices within the multidisciplinary artist community based at Somerset House, also functioning as a testing ground for new work, presenting commissions, premieres and live experiments that sit between exhibition and festival formats.

The opening night on Thursday, 26 March focuses on deep listening. Composer Ellen Arkbro will premiere For Crumhorns and Regal, a work created during her 2025 residency at Somerset House Studios, which will be performed by the London Crumhorn Consort with Arkbro accompanying on reed organ.

Artist and composer Raheel Khan will also present a new performance, Oh Forewarn – a score built around sustained brass tones and analogue synthesis, drawing on the historic use of horns as signalling devices.

An installation by musician Laurel Halo and artist Hanne Lippard will open the same evening in the Studios’ gallery space G31. Titled Sour Loop, the collaborative work combines musical composition and spoken language to explore how sound operates in public environments, drawing on references such as PA announcements, muzak and anti-loitering deterrent systems. The exhibition runs until 12 April.

On Friday, 27 March, Assembly presents two performance works shaped through improvisation and live sound processes. Somerset House Studios resident, PC Music’s felicita, will present czysta forma, an evolving live performance project inspired by the concept of “pure form” developed by Polish playwright and artist Witkacy. The work expands an electronic score into a live instrumental performance by a chamber ensemble.

The same evening will include Relay, a performance by Hannan Jones and Samir Kennedy that combines movement, looping tape systems and live sound capture.

Saturday, 28 March features eight performances alongside a programme of talks and lectures. The live programme begins with (un-)drowned by Seyi Adelekun, which blends experimental vocals, water percussion, movement and hydrophone recordings.

Later performances include Working Memory by interdisciplinary media artist Daniel Oduntan with the Audio Visual Ensemble, exploring how memory and place shift through regeneration and media circulation.

The afternoon will also feature new ensemble works by Turner Prize-winning artist Jasleen Kaur and artist-filmmaker Onyeka Igwe. Kaur’s Supra, developed during a year-long residency at Somerset House Studios, brings together an intergenerational group of musicians performing vocals, trumpet and dilruba. Igwe will present a live choral performance connected to their film installation, No archive can restore this chorus of (diasporic) shame.

Evening performances include a collaboration between composer Mark Fell and Iranian percussionist Mohammad Reza Mortazavi, as well as a new work by Hanne Lippard and Renato Grieco exploring voice through layered speech, music and viola da gamba.

The programme closes with performances by DeForrest Brown, Jr and Trevor Mathison. Brown will present a live iteration of Synoptic Audio, a multimedia project examining rhythm, spatial sound and the circulation of music. Mathison, a founding member of the Black Audio Film Collective, will present a new improvisational performance combining electronics, field recordings and environmental sound.

Tickets are available via Somerset House Studios now.

The post What to see at Somerset House Studios’ Assembly this month appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Omana Festival announces first 50 names
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival returns for its third edition this September in Kalamitsi.

The post Omana Festival announces first 50 names appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival returns for its third edition this September in Kalamitsi.

Omana Festival is set to return from 30 September – 6 October this year, once again taking over a secluded bay in Kalamitsi, Northern Greece. Tucked between forested hills and the open sea, the setting lies around two hours south of Thessaloniki.

The festival has now revealed the first 50 artists confirmed for 2026. Among them are Salamita, Dr Banana, Jane Fitz b2b Carl H, PARAMIDA, Mathew Jonson live, Anthea, Unai Trotti, XDB and more, with further artists still to be announced. The line-up brings together international names alongside local rising talent, maintaining strong ties to the Greek underground scene. Collaborations with local collectives such as Prism and Revolt, as well as platforms like Parisian distribution, record store and label Yoyaku will also shape the festival’s identity this year.

Omana’s musical direction focuses on extended DJ sets, giving artists the space to develop their sound over time, moving fluidly across minimal, house, techno, disco and downtempo.

Beyond the dancefloor, wellbeing remains central to the Omana experience. This year’s workshop programme draws inspiration from the elements and features more than 25 teachers and artists. Attendees can take part in yoga and breathwork sessions, experimental workshops and immersive ambient experiences designed to offer balance and reflection alongside the music.

Look out for more line-up announcements from Omana and get tickets here.

The post Omana Festival announces first 50 names appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Bristol New Music adds new names for 2026
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Bristol New Music will return this April with five days of performances, installations and cross-disciplinary projects.

The post Bristol New Music adds new names for 2026 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Bristol New Music will return this April with five days of performances, installations and cross-disciplinary projects.

Events will take place in a range of spaces across the city, including Arnolfini, Bristol Beacon, Spike Island, St George’s Bristol, Strange Brew, Watershed, The Cube, Kit Form and the Victoria Rooms.

The festival opens on 22 April at Strange Brew with US saxophonist and composer Cole Pulice, alongside a trio featuring guitarist Bill Orcutt, Sonic Youth drummer Steve Shelley and Comets on Fire bassist Ethan Miller.

The following evening, artist and DJ Olukemi Lijadu presents a newly commissioned live score to accompany her film Feedback at Spike Island, where the film installation is being exhibited during the festival.

Friday’s programme includes an improvisation-focused session at The Cube Microplex with Breton bagpiper Erwan Keravec and experimental duo @xcrswx, a.k.a. drummer Crystabel Riley and saxophonist Seymour Wright. Kenyan sound artist KMRU will perform at art institution Arnolfini, as well as audiovisual collaborators Rebecca Salvadori, Saint Abdullah and Eomac and Egyptian electronic artist YUNIS.

Saturday starts with an afternoon show at Arnolfini in collaboration Another Sky Festival with pianist Mark Knoop and London-based artist ãssia ghendir, before St George’s hosts acoustic performances from claire rousay with a Bristol ensemble, cellist Lucy Railton and the UK premiere of Harry Górski-Brown and Wojciech Rusin’s drone symphony Drift. The day will conclude at Bristol Beacon’s Lantern Hall with electronic sets from emptyset, GHOST DUBS, FRANKIE with Kelman Duran and Cara Tolmie & Rian Treanor.

The final day begins with contemporary repertoire from Shui Mo Ensemble at the Victoria Rooms, followed by performances blending from artists including Hatis Noit, Merope and Yas Clarke.

The closing event at Strange Brew features singer-pianist Eliana Glass, pianist Jasmine Wood with producer Pavel Milyakov, and UK experimental guitar collective Ex-Easter Island Head.

Festival passes are sold out, but day passes are available here.

The post Bristol New Music adds new names for 2026 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Jimi Jules releases new EP Baby Run on CircoLoco Records
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The Zürich-based DJ returns with his first multi-track project since 2023.

The post Jimi Jules releases new EP <i>Baby Run</i> on CircoLoco Records appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Zürich-based artist returns with his first multi-track project since 2023.

Jimi Jules has released a new three-track EP, Baby Run, marking his first appearance on CircoLoco Records. The project is also his first multi-track release since 2023’s Free Bird EP, and finds the Swiss DJ and multi-instrumentalist stretching across three distinct styles while keeping his signature sound intact.

At the centre is title track Baby Run, a club-ready cut built around live bass and layered vocal harmonies. Jules has described the track as a personal one, written years ago and rooted in the story of how he met his longtime partner.

The high-energy Turbo Rosso, first previewed in his CircoLoco Radio mix last year, ramps things up with a playful Italo-house edge, while Dreamin’ rounds out the EP with a steady nu-disco groove.

Listen to the project below.

The post Jimi Jules releases new EP <i>Baby Run</i> on CircoLoco Records appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Nuits sonores releases 2026 programme
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The French electronic festival is back in Lyon for its 23rd edition in May.

The post Nuits sonores releases 2026 programme appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The French electronic festival is back in Lyon for its 23rd edition in May.

Nuits sonores will once again take over a range of venues in the city of Lyon, France, from 13–17 May with a line-up that spans peak-time club sets, experimental live performances and immersive audiovisual presentations.

This year’s programme carries added significance, with the festival placing a clear focus on what shapes its identity: its status as an independent event.

The festival begins with a Day programme on Wednesday with sets from the likes of Anetha b2b Vel and Mac Declos b2b Ogazón, and a live performance of Heinali & Andriana-Yaroslava Saienko’s Гільдеґарда (Hildegarde). Also joining the opening day are Mala, dBridge and GEZAN.

From 10pm–5am, the Night programme continues with Von Bikräv, Gabber Eleganza b2b Yuta Umegatani present Manga Corps and more.

Thursday’s Day programme includes 999999999 (live), Skream and Benga, Craig Richards b2b Ivan Smagghe, Nikki Nair, Bogdan Raczynski, Kiss Facility and Lila Tirando a Violeta.

The Night programme features KITTIN & MCR-T, Carrier and Ben UFO.

A separate special concert at the Trinity Chapel on Thursday also welcomes Kali Malone, bringing a deeper, more meditative counterpoint to the weekend’s club intensity.

Friday’s Day programme features DJ Gigola b2b Busy P, HAAi b2b Leftfield, Djrum and gyrofield, while the Night selectors include Mochakk, OMOLOKO and DJ Travella.

Saturday’s Day programme spotlights a range of DJs including Deena Abdelwahed, before shifting into the night with GiGi FM, Sindh and Powell live.

The festival wraps on Sunday with a closing run featuring Batu and Leeza Protychenko, Sama’ Abdulhadi, Emma-Jean Thackray (live) and Dinamarca.

Alongside the music programme, Nuits sonores Lab will once again serve as the festival’s space for discussion and exchange. Its schedule brings together artists, labels, collectives, festivals, media and independent cultural thinkers, exploring current issues through talks, panels, workshops, interviews, radio sessions and masterclasses, with a special focus on the connections and culture that shape our independence.

Look back on our set recording mix series from Nuits sonores 2025.

See the full 2026 programme here and get tickets here.

The post Nuits sonores releases 2026 programme appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.content:encoded:
The festival is set to return to London’s Brockwell Park on Saturday, 23 May.

New names added to the Field Day 2026 bill include Partiboi69, Silva Bumpa and Larimae, joining previously revealed acts like Floating Points (live) and Honey Dijon.

Across the site, stages will once again spotlight a wide spectrum of electronic music.

The Bowl will welcome high-energy sets from KI/KI, Partiboi69, sim0ne and more.

Meanwhile, The Grove is set to bring together standout selectors including FOLD, Joy Orbison, Larimae, and a jungle set from Andy C.

Elsewhere, The Green will host Eliza Rose and a special Ewan McVicar b2b Special Request set, while The Pavilion will feature the likes of Anish Kumar and Saint Ludo.

Tickets are on sale now. Get yours here.

item.title: Tickets now live for V&A East Museum’s debut exhibition: The Music is Black: A British Story
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The exhibition opens on Saturday, 18 April 2026 at East Bank in London's Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.

The post Tickets now live for V&A East Museum’s debut exhibition: <i>The Music is Black: A British Story</i> appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The exhibition opens on Saturday, 18 April 2026 at East Bank in London’s Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.

Upcoming exhibition The Music is Black: A British Story will celebrate the voices, talent and stories of Black British music and its global impact over the past 125 years.

It also charts the development of Black British genres, including lovers rock, Brit funk, 2 tone, jungle, drum ‘n’ bass, trip-hop, UK garage, grime and more.

Designed as a multi-sensory experience, visitors will move through four themed acts supported by sound and multimedia installations, alongside 200+ objects from 1900 to the present day. The display includes 60 new acquisitions entering the V&A collection for the first time, from instruments and technologies to photography, fashion, personal items and artworks.

Objects on show include Winifred Atwell’s piano, Samuel Coleridge-Taylor’s early 1900s musical batons, and Jme’s Super Nintendo and Mario Paint, used in early music experiments before co-founding Boy Better Know with Skepta. The exhibition also includes clothing worn by Little Simz, Dame Shirley Bassey and Skin – the first Black woman to headline Glastonbury in 1997 – as well as photographs of Kemistry and Storm, Mis-Teeq and Skepta.

Structured in four parts – Origins of Music, ‘Great’ Britain: 1900–1969, British-Born Black Music, and The British Sound Of, which looks at Black British music today and its future – the exhibition moves from historical foundations through to contemporary sounds.

A partnership with BBC Music will provide access to archival materials and a related season of content across BBC platforms. The opening also marks the start of activity leading into The Music Is Black Festival, planned for summer 2026, with East Bank partners including the BBC, Sadler’s Wells East, UAL’s London College of Fashion and UCL East.

“This exhibition speaks to modernity and long deep histories; of the legacies of identity and to the music that furnishes our collective and individual memories,” said Jacqueline Springer, Curator of The Music Is Black: A British Story and Curator of Africa and Diaspora Performance at the V&A. We hope that visitors will emerge with a broader appreciation of Black British music makers, the enormous influence of Black musicality – internationally and domestically – and the legacy of the influence of the African diaspora.”

V&A East Museum opens on 18 April 2026, joining sister venue V&A East Storehouse, which opened in May 2025.

Get your tickets now at the V&A website.

The post Tickets now live for V&A East Museum’s debut exhibition: <i>The Music is Black: A British Story</i> appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Johnnie Walker and NTS announce three-day music programme Keep Walking Live: Manchester
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Five city-wide events feature artists including Anz, Space Afrika and Nubya Garcia.

The post Johnnie Walker and NTS announce three-day music programme Keep Walking Live: Manchester appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Five city-wide events feature artists including Anz, Space Afrika and Nubya Garcia, with 100 percent of net proceeds to be donated to local music charity Brighter Sound.

Scotch whisky brand Johnnie Walker has partnered with NTS to announce Keep Walking Live: Manchester, a three-day city-wide takeover celebrating pioneering music movements across the city. The programme runs from 13–15 March 2026.

Spanning some of Manchester’s most respected venues and cultural spaces – including SOUP, New Century, Eastern Bloc and Impiety Hour – the series brings together standout local names and internationally recognised artists, spotlighting the scenes that continue to shape the city’s musical identity.

The takeover begins on Friday, 13 March at Northern Quarter favourite SOUP. Focusing on UK dance culture, the line-up includes DJ EZ, Manchester’s own Anz,  NTS resident Finn, and emerging local talents KD22LR and Tyronwithnoe.

On Saturday, the programme opens with free, bookable DJ workshops at the NTS Manchester Studio, led by residents Finn and Abena. The afternoon then moves to Eastern Bloc for an intergenerational DJ showcase featuring Annabel Fraser, Kim Lana, and Rick.

That evening, KOKOROKO will headline historic venue New Century. The night will also feature saxophonist and producer Venna, a DJ set from Kusasa, and appearances from Charisse C and Captain Lulaz – both alumni of the Johnnie Walker x Dankie Sounds Bold Steps initiative.

The series closes on Sunday at Impiety Hour, the experimental space from the minds behind The White Hotel, for a community-focused ‘Sunday Slow Down’. The daytime programme includes listening sessions and panel discussions with local voices, leading into an intimate finale headlined by Nubya Garcia. She is joined by Space Afrika and Oriki.

With its emphasis on grassroots venues and community-led spaces, Keep Walking Live: Manchester aims to highlight the cultural ecosystems that sustain the city’s creative talent, and the curators and artists pushing its musical future forward.

In support of that mission, 100 percent of net proceeds from the events will go to Manchester music charity Brighter Sound, helping fund local artist development. Through community projects, international exchange and advocacy, Brighter Sound supports emerging talent and music professionals, challenging inequality and strengthening grassroots music networks.

Tickets are £5 for all events, and are available now via NTS.

Drink responsibly.

The post Johnnie Walker and NTS announce three-day music programme Keep Walking Live: Manchester appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Polifonic Puglia completes 2026 line-up
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Donato Dozzy, Ben UFO, Shackleton and more confirmed for this year’s edition.

The post Polifonic Puglia completes 2026 line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Donato Dozzy, Ben UFO, Shackleton and more confirmed for this year’s edition.

Polifonic Puglia has revealed the full programme for its 2026 return to Italy’s Valle d’Itria, with the festival set to run from Wednesday, 22 July to Sunday, 26 July.

This year’s theme, Sensory Bloom, frames the event as a heightened, full-spectrum celebration of the senses.

In total, 63 acts have been announced, spanning enduring legends, familiar Polifonic favourites and rising DJs.

Among the names confirmed are Ben UFO, Shackleton (live), Djrum, BASHKKA, Chloé Caillet, Peach, Ivan Smagghe, Pearson Sound, Sama’ Abdulhadi, ISAbella, Donato Dozzy, and Voices From The Lake (live).

Across the week, the festival will unfold at three key venues: Masseria Capece, Cala Maka, and Le Palme Beach Club.

Proceedings begin on Wednesday with an opening session at Cala Maka, before the action shifts from Thursday to Masseria Capece, which will host three consecutive days for the first time.

Polifonic 2026 wraps up on Sunday at Le Palme Beach Club, bringing the week to a close by the sea.

Tickets are available via DICE.

The post Polifonic Puglia completes 2026 line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
