item.title: Factory International unveils dance work honouring Sinéad O’Connor
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
A new production by Tony Award-winning choreographer Sonya Tayeh will feature in the spring 2026 season of premieres at Aviva Studios, alongside new visual art, theatre and music works.

The post Factory International unveils dance work honouring Sinéad O’Connor appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
A new production by Tony Award-winning choreographer Sonya Tayeh will feature in the spring 2026 season of premieres at Aviva Studios, alongside new visual art, theatre and music works.

Factory International has revealed plans for the world premiere of The Surge: An Ode to Sinéad O’Connor, a new dance production that will debut at Aviva Studios in Manchester from 25–27 June 2026. Created by Tony Award-winning choreographer and director Sonya Tayeh and presented in collaboration with The Joyce Theater in New York, the work marks the first major stage production devoted to the life and music of Sinéad O’Connor.

Conceived as a response to O’Connor’s uncompromising artistry and public defiance, The Surge explores themes of resistance, identity and spiritual reckoning. The piece is performed by an ensemble of ten women, whose collective presence challenges conventional ideas of ageing in dance. O’Connor’s recorded voice weaves through the performance, drawing on her songs as well as spoken excerpts from her memoir Rememberings, creating a sonic and emotional throughline for the choreography.

“Sinéad has been a part of my life since her first album arrived blaring out car windows in Detroit, where I’m from,” Sonya told Crack Magazine. “All of those memories came flooding back when she passed. I was heartbroken and had this sudden vision of ten women standing in a straight line at the edge of a stage and I was hearing Troy in my ears. That’s when I knew this was the next art quest and Sinéad is the path.”

“[She] is such an in-depth storyteller, such a poet, both in song and in text. Her writing has such an abstraction to it that feeds the way I see dance and the themes within this work. Even if not a linear path, both evoke such a strong point of view solely because they came from her,” she continued. “Playing Sinéad’s music in a dance studio does something to the psyche. It feels as though she’s saying, ‘Here’s where you pour it all out and see what’s left’. Joy and grief, strength and sorrow, faith and family, integrity and self-righteousness, and on and on.”

The premiere sits within Factory International’s spring 2026 programme at Aviva Studios, a season that brings together new work across visual art, performance and music. The wider line-up includes a major exhibition by Ai Weiwei, his first large-scale presentation in the North of England, alongside a newly commissioned performance piece by Sue Buckmaster and Gregory Maqoma, and the UK debut of the Pulitzer Prize-winning opera Angel’s Bone. Collectively, the programme reflects the organisation’s focus on ambitious, interdisciplinary work developed in partnership with international artists and institutions.

Aviva Studios will also host a music programme throughout the spring, spanning experimental and alternative genres as well as high-profile live shows, alongside a series of contemporary classical performances. Running parallel to the public programme, Factory International’s artist development schemes will continue to support emerging and early-career practitioners through training, mentoring and access to creative space.

Tickets for the spring 2026 season go on sale to members today (Tuesday, 20 January), followed by general release on Tuesday, 27 January.

For more information, visit Factory International’s website.

The post Factory International unveils dance work honouring Sinéad O’Connor appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: The Love Inn to celebrate 12th birthday with three days of parties this month
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The Bristol music venue is set to mark the occasion with a three-day run at the end of January.

The post The Love Inn to celebrate 12th birthday with three days of parties this month appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Bristol music venue is set to mark the occasion with a three-day run at the end of January.

After 12 years at the heart of Bristol’s underground, with Team Love firmly at the helm, The Love Inn has put together a celebratory extended weekender featuring familiar faces and sounds that have shaped the space.

Taking place across Thursday 29, Friday 30 and Saturday 31 January, the electronic venue will present 27 hours of music, uniting international selectors with long-standing members of its local family and reaffirming its reputation for thoughtful, community-led curation.

The celebrations begin on Thursday (10 pm–3 am) with a run of back-to-back sets from Freeman b2b K-Werk, Amber b2b Fern, Severine b2b Hella, ADDSOUND b2b Algy, and Dashy b2b Minnim b2b Heyza b2b AVA.

Friday (10 pm–5 am) sees sets from EMA, Ido Plumes, Jurango b2b Pluralist and Yushh, before Saturday’s extended finale (9 pm–12 am) welcomes Nicolas Lutz, Sugar Free, Paranoid London (DJ), Tia Cousins, Dave Harvey b2b Manfredas, plus R.E.D and Ellie Anderson.

Following a major refurbishment after its 10th birthday in 2024, the Stokes Croft institution now operates as a finely tuned 200-capacity space, complete with a fully upgraded Danley Audio sound system.

Get tickets via Headfirst for the Thursday, Friday, and Saturday, as well as on the door.

The post The Love Inn to celebrate 12th birthday with three days of parties this month appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Memòri Festival announces first edition and line-up
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
A new small-scale electronic music festival launches this July in southern France.

The post Memòri Festival announces first edition and line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
A new small-scale electronic music festival launches this July in southern France.

A new festival is joining the European summer calendar: Memòri Festival will debut from 2-5 July 2026 at the Domaine de Gayfié, near the village of Saint-Jean-de-Laur in the Lot region of southern France.

Founded by Antoine Hernandez, formerly involved in the programming of Positive Education and pe:rsona, alongside Quentin Courel of Underscope and Comic Sans Records, Memòri is conceived as a four-day gathering centred on exploratory electronic music in an intimate, rural setting. With a limited capacity of 2,000 people, the site sits on a limestone plateau surrounded by forests, streams and vineyards.

This year’s edition will feature four stages: a poolside sound system hosting DJ sets ranging from trip-hop to bass music and tech-house, a main stage dedicated to live acts and forward-leaning electronic performances, an ambient stage designed for rest and slow listening, and a barn converted into a nightclub for late-night dance sessions.

Balancing international names with French artists, the programming avoids headline-driven booking in favour of artists selected for their musical paths and the scenes they represent. So far, the line-up includes CCL, Shackleton (live), Mia Koden, Bambi and Beatrice M., Darwin, De Grandi (live), Azu Tiwaline & Tikiman (live), EMA, k means, and many more.

See the full programme here, and get tickets here.

The post Memòri Festival announces first edition and line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Duval Timothy and CJ Mirra collaborate on the film score for ‘My Father’s Shadow’
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The composers share first music from the upcoming film, with an official soundtrack release and live-score screening set for February 2026.

The post Duval Timothy and CJ Mirra collaborate on the film score for ‘My Father’s Shadow’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The composers share first music from the film, with an official soundtrack release and live-score screening set for February 2026.

Duval Timothy has joined forces with composer and sound designer CJ Mirra for his first full film soundtrack, written for the forthcoming My Father’s Shadow. Announced today, the pair have shared an early taste of the score with the release of the track Journey to Lagos.

The film marks the feature-length debut of director Akinola Davies Jr. and premiered at Cannes Un Certain Regard in 2025, where it made history as the first Nigerian production to appear in the festival’s official selection and received the Special Mention Caméra d’Or. Since then, it has gone on to collect 12 British Independent Film Award nominations – including Best Score and Best Sound – alongside multiple wins at The Gothams, and is the UK’s submission for Best International Feature Film at the 2026 Oscars.

Set across a single day during Nigeria’s 1993 election crisis, My Father’s Shadow follows an estranged father navigating Lagos with his two young sons as political unrest swells around them, turning a journey home into something far more precarious.

Across 12 tracks, Timothy and Mirra build a deeply expressive sonic world, the process of which began at the script stage, with Duval putting together early sketches in his Freetown studio and capturing field recordings while visiting the set in Ibadan.

With the film mostly seen through the eyes of Remi and Akin (played by brothers Chibuike Marvellous Egbo and Godwin Egbo), the score reflects a child’s sense of scale and wonder. Journey to Lagos depicts their first impressions of the city, while later pieces become more unsettled. Throughout, Roxanne Tataei’s voice recurs as an emotional echo of the boys’ absent mother. Sonically, it’s anchored by piano, which is played in both traditional and experimental ways, then shaped with vintage tape and early digital processing to echo the film’s period and 16mm atmosphere.

My Father’s Shadow – Official Motion Picture Soundtrack will be released on 6 February 2026 via Carrying Colour on vinyl, cassette and digital formats. The film opens in UK cinemas the same day.

A special live-score screening will follow on 7 February 2026 at Tate Modern’s South Tank, where the composers will perform the score alongside the film.

Tickets are available here. Watch the official trailer below.

The post Duval Timothy and CJ Mirra collaborate on the film score for ‘My Father’s Shadow’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Beyond The Valley drops official set times for its 10th anniversary edition
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Four days, multiple stages and a New Year’s Eve countdown – Beyond The Valley has unveiled when and where the action will unfold across its milestone 2025/26 festival.

The post Beyond The Valley drops official set times for its 10th anniversary edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Four days, multiple stages and a New Year’s Eve countdown – Beyond The Valley has unveiled when and where the action will unfold across its milestone 2025/26 festival.

Beyond The Valley has officially announced set times for its upcoming 10th anniversary edition, which kicks off this week from Sunday, 28 December – Thursday, 1 January.

On Sunday, the Dance Dome kicks off proceedings with a high-energy opening day featuring sets from Séarlait, Mon Franco, House Mum b2b Haus of Ralph, Afrodisiac b2b Baby G, Kilimanjaro, Bullet Tooth and plenty more. TV Rock will close out the night, setting the pace for the days ahead.

On Monday, attention shifts to the Valley Stage, where performances from the likes of 2Charm, DICE, JoJo and 070 Shake lead into a late-night slot from pop girl of the moment, Addison Rae. A Welcome to Country will also take place at the Valley Stage at 4:15pm, with attendees encouraged to attend in respect.

Over in the Dance Dome, the energy ramps up with BL3SS, Prospa, Chris Stussy, Juicy Romance, DJ Heartstring and more.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Beyond The Valley (@beyondthevalleyfest)

Tuesday is set to be another stacked day across the site. The Valley Stage will host artists such as The Tullamarines, Boy Soda, glaive, Balu Brigada, Channel Tres and Spacey Jane, while the Dance Dome welcomes RONA., Jazzy, Inside Kru, Patrick Mason and more, before I Hate Models brings the night to a close.

The festival culminates on Wednesday with returning artist Dom Dolla headlining the Valley Stage with the official countdown set from 11:59pm to 2:00am. In the run-up, catch performances from the likes of Chloe Parché, Kaiit, Julia Wolf, Jane Remover, Fcukers, Turnstile and Kid Cudi.

Across the final night, the Dance Dome will host sets from Tina Disco, Cassian, VTSS, Bella Claxton and Sim0ne, before SWIM closes out the festival with a final send-off.

Beyond the music, this year the festival will also present an updated mix of on-site experiences. The Jack Daniel’s House Party, POOF DOOF’s Cirque Du Slay, Red Bull Unforeseen and Smirnoff Schmall Klub return to create standout moments across Downtown BTV, while new additions include The Sanctuary presented by QV Ceramides – offering ice baths, yoga, pilates and meditation – alongside The Lounge Room, home to the new Salomon Run Club and live podcasts curated by The Daily Aus.

Festival-goers can also now download the newly launched BTV App, powered by Woov and available via the App Store and Google Play, which will house the full BTV25 Information Guide alongside interactive maps, real-time updates and info on secret sets.

See the full artist programme here.

The post Beyond The Valley drops official set times for its 10th anniversary edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: The Collections, Vol. V is here featuring Oklou, David Byrne, Nemzzz & more
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Our fifth anthology gathers 11 2025 cover stories and two years of defining imagery into a permanent archive of music and culture.

The post <i>The Collections, Vol. V</i> is here featuring Oklou, David Byrne, Nemzzz & more appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Our fifth anthology gathers 11 2025 cover stories and two years of defining imagery into a permanent archive of music and culture.

The Collections, Vol. V is our collectable anthology that brings together all of Crack Magazine’s 2025 cover stories – including Oklou, Thundercat, Nemzzz, Noname and David Byrne – alongside imagery from our most memorable shoots of the past two years.

Spanning underground pace-setters to art rock titans, Vol. V captures the visual language of the year’s most influential artists in high definition. Printed on premium heavy-stock paper, this is a tactile piece of music history – a permanent home for the cultural moments that defined 2025.

This fifth volume is made possible thanks to the generosity of Roland and our community of Supporters.

Pre-order The Collections, Vol. V here now.

The post <i>The Collections, Vol. V</i> is here featuring Oklou, David Byrne, Nemzzz & more appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Listen to GbClifford’s latest, ‘Mock the World 2gether’
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The Prague-based artist’s new album deals with temporality, presence, and the search for meaning within overstimulated modern life.

The post Listen to GbClifford’s latest, ‘Mock the World 2gether’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Prague-based artist’s new album deals with temporality, presence, and the search for meaning within overstimulated modern life.

SHAPE+ Platform affiliate GbClifford has released a new album, Mock the World 2gether, out now via Wrong.

On the project, the sound collage and experimental pop artist explores finding presence in a constantly shifting digital world, layering original compositions with telephone recordings and other audio snippets to create a listening experience that feels both intimate and unstable. While Mock the World 2gether leans more heavily into pop ballads than previous work, those moments are filtered through distorted sounds and intentionally unpolished edits.

Structured as two distinct chapters, the record follows an emotional arc. The first, Sometimes I Think About, centres on disconnection and overload, while the second chapter shifts toward nostalgia, drawing on memories of shopping malls, early vlogging culture, and familiar television programming. These scenes echo the internet’s fascination with liminal spaces, reframing environments often seen as empty or obsolete as places still capable of holding warmth and liveliness.

Listen below.

The post Listen to GbClifford’s latest, ‘Mock the World 2gether’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Nuits sonores unveils first wave of artists for its 2026 edition
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff

The post Nuits sonores unveils first wave of artists for its 2026 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The electronic festival returns to Lyon next summer from 13-17 May.

Nuits sonores has dropped the first names for its 23rd edition, revealing over 50 acts and 70 artists set to play the festival’s daytime programmes and its traditional Closing Day. The 2026 edition will run from 13 to 17 May in Lyon, France.

This initial wave spans live performances and DJ sets from across the electronic spectrum, including Vril & Wata Igarashi (live), DJ Gigola, gyrofield, Kiss Facility (live), Mala, Sama’ Abdulhadi, Lila Tirando a Violeta (live), Nikki Nair, Anetha b2b Vel, dBridge and Dinamarca.

Nuits sonores will continue its rollout early next year, with the full Days and Closing Day line-up, along with the complete Nights programme, set to be announced in January.

Read our review of the 2025 edition here, listen to a series of set recordings from the festival here, and get tickets for the 2026 edition here.

Check out the full line-up so far below.

item.title: We’ve teamed up with MPB to commission photo series from three emerging photographers
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Irene Haro, Jock Thomson and Mariana Pires have received a £1,000 grant to develop original photo stories focused on the communities and cultural spaces that matter most to them.

The post We’ve teamed up with MPB to commission photo series from three emerging photographers appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Irene Haro, Jock Thomson and Mariana Pires have received a £1,000 grant to develop original photo stories focused on the communities and cultural spaces that matter most to them.

As part of the project, MPB, the global platform for buying, selling and trading used photo and video equipment, provided each photographer with loaned gear to support the creation of their work. The collaboration aims to remove financial barriers, empowering early-career creatives to experiment, refine their process, and tell ambitious visual stories.

The resulting projects span three distinct corners of the UK’s creative landscape. In London, Mariana Pires turns her lens to the Paraiso School of Samba. Her photo essay captures the energy of rehearsals, the group’s deep-rooted community spirit, and the behind-the-scenes labour that powers its year-round programme. Launching on 3 December, it will be accompanied by a recorded voiceover and behind-the-scenes footage.

In Glasgow, Jock Thomson documents the city’s underground queer rave scene, offering an intimate and atmospheric portrait of the spaces where community, resistance and nightlife culture converge. His story will be published on 10 December.

Closing the series is Irene Haro, who focuses on the reopening of Bristol venue The Island. Now led by an all-female management team, the venue’s story becomes the centre of a project exploring renewal, representation and the importance of independent cultural spaces. Her series will go live on 17 December.

Look out for each story launching on crackmagazine.net.

The post We’ve teamed up with MPB to commission photo series from three emerging photographers appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Rewire announces first artists and projects for 2026
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Smerz, Joanne Robertsonf and Blawan are among the names announced for the festival's 15th edition.

The post Rewire announces first artists and projects for 2026 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Smerz, Joanne Robertson and Blawan are among the names announced for the festival’s 15th edition.

Rewire has announced the first 44 artists and projects for its 15th edition, which will return to The Hague from 9-12 April.

Taking place across over 20 venues, including concert halls, theatres, churches and nightclubs in the city centre of The Hague, the festival promises four days of sonic exploration from emerging talent and era-defining musicians. 

The first announcement features performances from Oneohtrix Point Never, as well as a wave of collaborative centrepieces: Caterina Barbieri with ONCEIM, Actress & Suzanne Ciani’s Concrète Waves, Sumac & Moor Mother, Beatrice Dillon & Explore Ensemble, and a rare performance from Beverly Glenn-Copeland and Elizabeth Copeland.

Continuing to push its ethos of a boundary-pushing and leftfield programme, other highlights include Civilistjävel! & Mayssa Jallad, Joanne Robertson & Oliver Coates, Cara Tolmie & Rian Treanor, Purelink with Mia Oki, Økse & billy woods, Eraserhead x Xiu Xi, Armand Hammer, Blawan (Live), Malibu, James K, Fine, and KAVARI (Live).

Individual day passes, as well as three-day and four-day passes, are available now via the Rewire website.

The post Rewire announces first artists and projects for 2026 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Open’er Festival announces second wave of artists for its 2026 edition
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
JADE, IDLES and Clipse have joined the line-up for the festival’s 23rd edition, which will take place from 1-4 July in Gdynia.

The post Open’er Festival announces second wave of artists for its 2026 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
IDLES, Clipse and JADE have joined the line-up for the festival’s 23rd edition, which will take place from 1-4 July in Gdynia.

Open’er Festival returns to Poland’s Baltic Coast from 1-4 July with a line-up spanning pop, rock, post-punk, hip-hop and more. 

New additions to the line-up include Bristol-based post-punk band and former Crack Magazine cover stars IDLES, recently reunited American hip-hop duo Clipse (comprised of brothers Pusha T and Malice), and JADE.

Other highlights include Swedish pop hitmaker Zara Larsson, singer and actress Reneé Rapp, and alternative R&B artist Audrey Nuna.

These artists join already announced artists, including Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, and former Crack Magazine cover stars David Byrne and Ethel Cain. 

Tickets and information are available through Open’er Festival’s website.

The post Open’er Festival announces second wave of artists for its 2026 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Montreux Jazz Festival and Southbank Centre announce 2026 residency celebrating Miles Davis
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
From 13-15 March 2026, London’s Southbank Centre will explore the question ‘What is jazz today?’ through a series of performances and talks.

The post Montreux Jazz Festival and Southbank Centre announce 2026 residency celebrating Miles Davis appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
From 13-15 March 2026, London’s Southbank Centre will explore the question ‘What is jazz today?’ through a series of performances and talks.

The residency, now in its second year, asks the question, ‘What is jazz today?’ and celebrates the life and career of jazz’s most influential and acclaimed figure, Miles Davis, a century after his birth. 

On Friday, 13 March, trumpeter, composer, producer and bandleader Theo Croker will discuss and reinterpret key jazz pieces that have influenced him over the years. Five Points Project, a community that looks to alleviate loneliness through live music, improvisation, and open collaboration, will bring its multi-sensory celebration to the Montreux Jazz Festival.

Friday’s programme also features Women in Jazz – an organisation that celebrates the music and stories of women in jazz to inspire the next generation of talent. 

Saturday’s programme welcomes music journalist Emma Warren for a talk with English singer-songwriter Celeste about her musical journey. Live storytelling event OneTrackMinds will also present Hidden Tracks, for which a selection of storytellers will discuss how one song or piece of music has changed their life, with the goal to inspire and help people develop their own stories. Saturday’s events end with a performance from Manchester soul and hip-hop duo Children of Zeus, with support from Tomorrow’s Warriors. 

Sunday opens with a Hidden Tracks creative writing workshop, giving attendees the tools to write creatively about a piece of music, followed by a series of guest artists discussing different aspects of D’Angelo’s work and how it shaped their music practice. Multi-instrumentalist, composer and producer corto.alto will perform his improvised live set, and Swiss-Palestinian drummer Ramzi Hammad will play music exploring how migration has influenced jazz today.

More information and individual tickets to each event are available via the Southbank Centre’s website.

The post Montreux Jazz Festival and Southbank Centre announce 2026 residency celebrating Miles Davis appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Pulp, CMAT, and Mac DeMarco to headline End of the Road 2026
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival is set to return to Larmer Tree Gardens for its 20th anniversary edition next September.

The post Pulp, CMAT, and Mac DeMarco to headline End of the Road 2026 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival is set to return to Larmer Tree Gardens for its 20th anniversary edition next September.

Today (20 November), End of the Road Festival announced its 2026 edition, which will take place in Dorset’s  Larmer Tree Gardens in Dorset from 3-6 September.

Pulp, CMAT and Mac DeMarco are set to headline, alongside a secret headliner on Thursday.

End of the Road welcomes around 15,000 attendees each year to experience hundreds of live performances, comedy, literature, and a range of independent food and drink stalls. Its loyal fan base, eclectic line-up and eco-friendly initiatives have enabled the festival to continue for 20 years, offering an alternative to larger music festivals. 

Previous years have welcomed artists like Black Country, New Road, John Maus, Joy Orbison, Pixies, King Krule, Khruangbin and Little Simz.

Visit the End of the Road’s website for tickets and more information.

The post Pulp, CMAT, and Mac DeMarco to headline End of the Road 2026 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: DJ and producer Jamback makes his debut on CircoLoco Records with ‘Positive’
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Dutch artist Jamback transforms personal loss into a powerful, club-ready track.

The post DJ and producer Jamback makes his debut on CircoLoco Records with ‘Positive’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Dutch artist Jamback transforms personal loss into a powerful, club-ready track.

Jamback lands on CircoLoco Records with Positive, a stripped-back yet emotionally charged track shaped by the loss of his mother. “Everything felt heavy, and I needed to find a way to turn that pain into movement. It’s a journey through grief and rough edges, but also a release – and eventually, joy,” he says. “I hope people can feel that emotional shift when they hear it.”

Originally circulating as an underground ID, the track was picked up by DJs around the world, with Chris Stussy opening his first Coachella set of 2025 with it and further support coming from Marco Carola, Jamie Jones, East End Dubs, Sidney Charles and Toman. 

After the song debuted on Beatport at number one, his current campaign stretches across the UK, Europe, Asia, and Australia, with upcoming shows in Hong Kong and Bali, and an Australian tour at the end of November.

The post DJ and producer Jamback makes his debut on CircoLoco Records with ‘Positive’ appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: John Talabot and Richie Hawtin set to headline Polifonic Milano
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival returns to Milan from 20-23 November with the theme ‘City Echoes’.

The post John Talabot and Richie Hawtin set to headline Polifonic Milano appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival returns to Milan from 20-23 November with the theme ‘City Echoes’.

Over four days in Milan, venues Alcatraz, Basic Village, Mogo Hi-Fi, and, for the first time, ADI Design Museum, are set to host a range of musical performances, workshops, club nights, and immersive activities. 

Previously taking place at the art and design museum Triennale di Milano, Polifonic will now take over the -1 hall of the prestigious ADI Design Museum – a move that reflects Polifonic’s goal to pursue singular locations that amplify the festival’s sensory and visual identity.

The venue is set to host an Ableton workshop with producers Flore and Piezo, as well as a short documentary titled New Ghost, presented by NR Magazine and directed by Johannes Haeussler, which will explore the roots and culture of Chicago footwork. 

Among the music highlights are headline shows at Milan club and venue Alcatraz from Spanish DJ and producer John Talabot, and an Italian exclusive performance of DEX EFX X0X from electronic artist Richie Hawtin.

Other highlights include sets from Flore b2b Piezo, e/tape, Franesco Del Garda, Mun Sing, Sister Effect, and Or:la, as well as a listening session with Nicola Mazzetti, and a live painting performance from Conxi Sane. 

Visit the Polifonic website for more information and tickets.

The post John Talabot and Richie Hawtin set to headline Polifonic Milano appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Tickets for Field Maneuvers 2026 are now live
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Field Maneuvers will return to a secret location in Norfolk from 21-23 August next year.

The post Tickets for Field Maneuvers 2026 are now live appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Field Maneuvers will return to a secret location in Norfolk from 21-23 August next year.

After an end-of-summer early bird presale sold out in under two hours, independent festival Field Maneuvers has released the general ticket sale for its 2026 edition.

While the full line-up is yet to be announced, Field Maneuvers promises to deliver a programme that goes against the standard club-world grain, as well as artist mixes throughout the lead-up to the festival.

The festival’s 2025 instalment delivered a range of unforgettable sets, including sets from JASSS b2b Yazzus, Parris, Paurro, Compliments, aya, ISAbella, Midland, Cooly G, and Kode9. 

A limited number of Access Tickets are also available each year to help keep FM as inclusive and accessible as possible. These tickets are reserved for NHS staff, people affected by strike action, or anyone with a low income or currently facing financial hardship. To apply, email accesstickets@fieldmaneuvers.com with a short note explaining your situation, and the team will be in touch.

For more information and to purchase tickets, visit the Field Maneuvers website.

The post Tickets for Field Maneuvers 2026 are now live appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: SHAPE+ Platform affiliates SWAI unveil new music video for their track Globoko
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
'Globoko' is on the duo's most recent album, 'V drevo', which was released on Clockwork Voltage earlier this year.

The post SHAPE+ Platform affiliates SWAI unveil new music video for their track <i>Globoko</i> appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Globoko is on the duo’s most recent album, V drevo, which was released on Clockwork Voltage earlier this year.

SWAI is a techno project by two veterans of the Slovenian electronic music scene: Shekuza, also known as Miha Šajina, and BeatMyth, also known as Igor Vuk, who are members of the Ljubljana-based label Clockwork Voltage. As solo artists, the two have decades of experience as producers, songwriters and live performers, working on multiple solo and collaborative projects primarily in the world of modular synthesis and contemporary electronic music. 

The duo has now released a new music video for their track Globoko, from their 2025 album V Drevo. The video, directed by Izak Cunta, is composed of recontextualised archival footage that reflects on the increasing isolation of individuals and the uneasy closeness between humans and technology.

Watch the video below.

 

 

The post SHAPE+ Platform affiliates SWAI unveil new music video for their track <i>Globoko</i> appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Don Julio and Gabbriette are throwing a Día de Muertos party inside a London bakery
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Mexico City’s cult bakery Panadería Rosetta and Handshake Speakeasy join forces for a three-day takeover of Toklas Bakery in honour of Día de Muertos.

The post Don Julio and Gabbriette are throwing a Día de Muertos party inside a London bakery appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Mexico City’s cult bakery Panadería Rosetta and Handshake Speakeasy join forces for a three-day takeover of Toklas Bakery in honour of Día de Muertos.

This Halloween weekend, London gets a dose of Mexico City energy as Don Julio Tequila transforms Toklas Bakery into Panadería Don Julio.

Running from 31 October to 2 November, the three-day event folds together Mexico’s culinary and club scenes. By day, guests can sample pan de muerto – the sweet, symbolic bread baked by chef Elena Reygadas of Panadería Rosetta – alongside Don Julio cocktail workshops and a Milagros-curated bodega filled with handmade Mexican crafts.

At night, the bakery will transform into an 80-person speakeasy, led by Mexico City’s Handshake Speakeasy. With Don Julio cocktails available all night, the menu will include a new Paloma designed by model and musician Gabbriette, who will host the Thursday night launch.

The weekend’s music program spans reggaetón, techno, and experimental club sounds, with sets from Mexico City’s N.A.A.F.I collective, CC:DISCO!, and London’s queer-Latinx crew 2CPERREA.

On Sunday, the official Day of the Dead, guests can take part in nail art, face painting, and other rituals set against traditional papel picado décor and a marigold-filled ofrenda centerpiece, rounding out a weekend of reflection and revelry.

Kandace Williamson, Don Julio’s Senior Culture Lead, said the project aims to bring “the spirit of modern Mexico – bold, creative, and deeply connected to its roots – to new audiences.”

Sign up for the Don Julio Tequila x Panadería Rosetta: Cocktail Making Workshop here and explore the full line-up for the speakeasy takeover here.

The post Don Julio and Gabbriette are throwing a Día de Muertos party inside a London bakery appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Mix With The Masters is offering five new masterclasses before the end of 2025
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Mix With The Masters, an award-winning platform for music makers, is set to host a new series of masterclasses at Rue Boyer Studios in Paris.

The post Mix With The Masters is offering five new masterclasses before the end of 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Five industry-leading mix engineers are heading to Paris to host a series of unmissable music masterclasses.

Mix With The Masters, an award-winning platform for music makers, is set to host a new series of masterclasses at Rue Boyer Studios in Paris. These events connect top industry professionals with aspiring creators seeking to advance their skills and careers across all areas of music production.

This year, MWTM has already hosted nine masterclasses, including sessions with Leslie Brathwaite [Pharrell Williams, Beyoncé], David Wrench [Frank Ocean, Bon Iver] and Rich Costey [Haim, sombr].

To facilitate access to their events, the organization has renewed its ReBalance Scholarship programme. This initiative fights to address gender imbalance in recording studios by offering female and non-binary applicants up to 50 percent off the cost of their on-site seminars and masterclasses.

The next three months are set to be the biggest yet for Mix With The Masters, as they are set to host five new masterclasses with an impressive lineup of engineers:

– Jon Castelli (27–31 October) – mixing engineer for chart-topping artists including SZA and Billie Eilish – will lead a week of high-level studio workshops.

– Alan Meyerson (3–7 November) – renowned for his immersive mixing work on Dune and Interstellar – will teach a Dolby Atmos score mixing masterclass.

– Nk.F (17–21 November) – a leading figure in French-speaking music – will host an intensive five-day mixing program.

– Bainz (8–10 December) – the mixing engineer behind Young Thug, Gunna and Lil Baby – will offer a focused three-day masterclass.

– Ben Baptie (15–17 December) – who has worked with The Strokes, Little Simz, and Moses Sumney – will close out the year’s series with a technical mixing masterclass.

Find out more about Mix With The Masters and how to apply to each masterclass here.

 

The post Mix With The Masters is offering five new masterclasses before the end of 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Dolby to showcase immersive sound design across two locations at ADE 2025
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Taking place from 22–24 October, the programme will feature immersive listening sessions and artist talks at the Dolby Atmos House, as well as hands-on workshops at ADE Lab.

The post Dolby to showcase immersive sound design across two locations at ADE 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Taking place from 22–24 October, the programme will feature immersive listening sessions and artist talks at the Dolby Atmos House, as well as hands-on workshops at ADE Lab.

Dolby is set to deliver an immersive audio experience at this year’s Amsterdam Dance Event (ADE), taking place from 22–24 October 2025. Across two locations – TOBACCO Theatre and ADE Lab – Dolby will highlight the creative and technical possibilities of Dolby Atmos, bringing together artists, producers and fans for three days of sound innovation.

On Thursday the 23rd and Friday the 24th, Dolby Atmos House at TOBACCO Theatre will serve as an immersive listening hub, featuring a series of exclusive sessions and artist talks in the heart of Amsterdam.

Boundary-pushing creators, including Max Cooper, Nadia Struiwigh, Armin van Buuren, Don Diablo and Jeff Mills will explore how Dolby Atmos enhances their creative process. Attendees can experience these artists’ music in the multidimensional sound of Dolby Atmos, and gain insight into how immersive audio is shaping the future of music production and performance.

For the first time, Dolby Creator Lab will also host technical, hands-on workshops at ADE Lab, starting on Wednesday, 22 October in Westerpark. Designed for both beginners and intermediate-level creatives, these sessions will dive into the fundamentals of Dolby Atmos production, covering both technical and artistic perspectives. From 23–24 October, the Dolby Creator Lab will move to STMPD Studios, where selected participants can take part in intimate artist deep dives and studio sessions led by renowned STMPD Studio engineers and mixers.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Amsterdam Dance Event (@amsterdamdanceevent)


The Dolby Atmos House at TOBACCO Theatre will be free and open to the public throughout the ADE Festival. Attendees can also experience Dolby Atmos on the move with the Mercedes-Benz shuttle service, and travel between Dolby Atmos House, ADE Lab, and STMPD Studios while immersed in spatial audio.

For more information and to view the full schedule, visit the official ADE website.

The post Dolby to showcase immersive sound design across two locations at ADE 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Einhundert announces CODE.SWITCH – a three-day programme exploring club culture
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
The programme will take place 17-19 October at the Adidas Sports Base in Berlin.

The post Einhundert announces CODE.SWITCH – a three-day programme exploring club culture appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The free event will take place from 17-19 October at the Adidas Sports Base in Berlin.

Built around three thematic pillars – sound, silence, and movement – each day of CODE.SWITCH focuses on one of these core aspects of the club experience. The program features live performances, DJ sets, lectures and listening sessions from a range of collaborators, including Cktrl, Scratchclart, Nico Adomako and more. 

Split across three days, the event will host a sound lecture with Uruguayan DJ and producer Lechuga Zafiro, with accompanying DJ sets from Nazar and Nunguja on day one. Day two will see a production workshop from Jordanian DJ and sound designer Toumba, with sets from Dengue Dengue Dengue, Dis Fig, Mobilegirl, Suutoo, and Scratcha DVA feat. Dark Man Zulu. The final day will host a Yegorka listening session with Berlin and Copenhagen-based producer and DJ Why Be, with sets from Cktrl, NAS TEA, Primitive Art, and Yantan Ministry.

Individual free entry passes for each day of CODE.SWITCH can be reserve here.

The post Einhundert announces CODE.SWITCH – a three-day programme exploring club culture appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Jen Cardini and AEG Presents France announce the inaugural Nightclubbing Festival in Paris
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
From 24-27 September, Paris will host the first edition of Nightclubbing Festival – a celebration of electronic music, nightlife culture, and the political power of the dance floor.

The post Jen Cardini and AEG Presents France announce the inaugural Nightclubbing Festival in Paris appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
From 24-27 September, Paris will host the first edition of Nightclubbing Festival – a celebration of electronic music, nightlife culture, and the political power of the dance floor.

Jen Cardini and AEG Presents France have unveiled the first-ever edition of Nightclubbing Festival, set to take place in Paris from 24-27 September 2026. Expanding on Cardini’s renowned Nightclubbing party series and platform, the festival marks the next evolution of this idea. 

Centred at La Villette and extending across multiple clubs and cultural venues, the event will feature a mix of live performances, discussions, installations, and collaborations. Thematically, they will examine how nightlife can shape more equitable and inclusive futures, inspired by the political and cultural legacy of club culture.

Before the festival takes place in September 2026, Nightclubbing will return to its roots in some much-loved clubs around Europe: Virage in Paris (18 October), San Francisco Bar in Amsterdam (23 October), RSO in Berlin (1 November), and Outra Cena in Lisbon (29 November). 

First festival line-up announcements and ticket sales will be revealed later this year.

The post Jen Cardini and AEG Presents France announce the inaugural Nightclubbing Festival in Paris appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Polifonic Milano announces line-up and concept for 2025
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival will take place across multiple venues in Milan from 20-23 November.

The post Polifonic Milano announces line-up and concept for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival will take place across multiple venues in Milan from 20-23 November.

The fourth edition of Polifonic Milano returns to the city with the concept ‘City Echoes’.

Over four days, the venues Alcatraz, Basic Village, Mogo Hi-Fi, Triennale CUORE and Triennale VOCE will host a rich programme of live performances, club nights, workshops, talks, and more.

Music highlights include sets from E/Tape, Flore b2b Piezo, Francesco del Garda, Leo Mas, Or:la and John Talabot. Richie Hawtin will also present his DEX EFX X0X project exclusively in Italy, and Mun Sing is set to deliver a powerful and unpredictable live performance.

Other notable projects on the programme include Frontiers of Light and Space: Approaching Environments with Anonima Luci – a talk that will explore he dialogue between light and architecture, and a live painting performance from multidisciplinary Spanish artist Conxi Sane.

Visit the Polifonic website for more information and tickets.

 

The post Polifonic Milano announces line-up and concept for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Le Guess Who? releases full concert film of Darkside’s 2024 festival performance
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The trio’s set is now available to stream, adding to the festival’s ever-growing archive of performances.

The post Le Guess Who? releases full concert film of Darkside’s 2024 festival performance appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The trio’s set, featuring material from their latest album Nothing alongside older favourites, is now available to stream, adding to the festival’s ever-growing archive of performances.

Le Guess Who? has released the full concert film of Darkside’s performance from its 2024 edition, capturing the trio’s immersive, genre-bending live show in Utrecht.

Since their debut album Psychic, Darkside – made up of multi-instrumentalist Dave Harrington, electronic producer Nicolás Jaar, and, more recently, drummer Tlacael Esparza – have carved out a singular space in contemporary music by fusing electronic experimentation with psychedelic pop, prog rock, disco and more. The group served as one of the guest curators of Le Guess Who? 2024 and took to the stage just ahead of releasing their latest album, Nothing.

The newly released concert film, directed by Dammes Kieft with audio recorded by Marc Broer and mixed by Darkside themselves, features a sweeping setlist that moves between early favourites like Paper Trails and fresh material from Nothing, including Hell Suite, Pt. 1 and Graucha Max.

The release joins the Le Guess Who? Recordings archive, which now houses more than 120 performances from past editions, freely available online. The collection includes sets by artists such as Kim Gordon, Meshell Ndegeocello, Stereolab, Aldous Harding, clipping., Art Ensemble of Chicago, The Microphones, Linton Kwesi Johnson, and Beverly Glenn-Copeland.

With its motto Listening is the Way Forward, Le Guess Who? continues to position itself as one of the world’s most adventurous music festivals, spotlighting voices that challenge and expand what’s possible in sound.

Le Guess Who? 2025 will take place from 6–9 November in Utrecht, The Netherlands. More information and remaining tickets are available at the festival’s website.

Watch Darkside’s full performance below.

The post Le Guess Who? releases full concert film of Darkside’s 2024 festival performance appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Ableton Live 12 Suite is now available on rent-to-own
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
The new rent-to-own plan allows customers to significantly reduce the upfront cost of Ableton's fully-featured music production software.

The post Ableton Live 12 Suite is now available on rent-to-own appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The new rent-to-own plan allows customers to significantly reduce the upfront cost of Ableton’s fully-featured music production software.

Music software company Ableton have announced an all-new rent-to-own plan for Ableton Live 12 Suite. Customers can spread the cost of the music production software to significantly decrease the upfront cost, and unlike other subscription services, own the license in full after all payments have been made. 

Through the Ableton webshop, users will be able to spread the regular cost of the software over 24 monthly instalments for Live 12 Suite licenses and 12 monthly instalments for Live 12 Suite EDU licenses, with no extra charges. 

The rent-to-own plan can be paused at any time without incurring any additional charges. Users will retain access to the software until the end of the current billing cycle, and the remaining balance can be paid off at any time. If a new version of the software is released while a user’s rent-to-own plan is still active, they’ll have the option to extend the amount of monthly payments to cover the cost of the upgrade.

Learn more about Ableton Live 12 here.

The post Ableton Live 12 Suite is now available on rent-to-own appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: The Warehouse Project announces full line-up for Repercussion 2025
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
First winter edition of WHP staple will take place on December 13 and feature Underworld, Caribou, Floating Points, SHERELLE and more.

The post The Warehouse Project announces full line-up for Repercussion 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
First winter edition of WHP event will take place on December 13 and feature Underworld, Caribou, Floating Points, SHERELLE and more.

The Warehouse Project’s calendar staple, Repercussion, is back again for 2025, this time with a winter edition. Known for its eclectlic statement line-ups, the season favourite will bring a mixture of electronic music royalty and exciting new selectors to Manchester’s Mayfield Depot, a stone’s throw from Piccadilly Station. 

Taking place on Saturday 13 December, the first winter edition of Repercussion will see live performances from Underworld and Caribou, as well as sets from Floating Points (who will play solo and B2B alongside Daphni), Joy Orbison, Special Request and SHERELLE. The event will take over the entirety of the cavernous Mayfield Depot site as well as spilling out into the adjoining Star & Garter pub and Freight Island’s Plant Room. 

Highlights elsewhere on the bill include Crack Mix alumni Chloé Caillet, p-rallel, PARAMIDA, Call Super and Ryan Elliot, as well as sets from Shanti Celeste, Daphni and Job Jobse. 

Pre-sale ticket sales go live at 9am Friday 3rd October. General ticket sales go live at 12 noon Friday 3rd October. Sign up at www.repercussion.uk.com for pre-sale access.

The post The Warehouse Project announces full line-up for Repercussion 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: SHAPE+ Platform affiliate Pizza Noise Mafia set to release new album
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Pizza Noise Mafia are a Brussels-based electronic duo and SHAPE+ Platform 25/26 artists, made up of Matthieu Levet and Thibault Gondard. The album, titled Il Grande Ritorno, is set for release on Slaaploos on 8 November.

The post SHAPE+ Platform affiliate Pizza Noise Mafia set to release new album appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The album, titled Il Grande Ritorno, is set for release on Slaaploos on 8 November.

Pizza Noise Mafia are a Brussels-based electronic duo and SHAPE+ Platform 25/26 artists, made up of Matthieu Levet and Thibault Gondard. Operating under the profile of funky minimal 6-step, the duo ause only a drum machine and a synthesiser to make rhythmic, stripped-back, groove-driven productions. 

On 8 November, Pizza Noise Mafia are set to release their new album Il Grande Ritorno. The album will mark 10 years since the duo’s debut album i canti della malavita, released on Belgian record label Slaaploos in 2014. Since then, the duo has built a relentless live reputation, implementing a minimalist setup that has been proven at iconic venues and festivals across France and Belgium, as well as illegal basement raves.

Il Grande Ritorno is described as “the result of ten years of all-out research in the field of dance music”. Rooted in Belgian and Brussels tradition, the album blends a variety of dance music genres from trance, EBM, hardcore, electro, and acid to create a sound that is uniquely theirs.

Before the release of the album, the duo will be performing multiple shows across France, including Virieu-le-Grand (27 September), Genève (28 September), Grenoble (30 September), and Marseille (4 October). 

Alongside French producer Heimat, they will also perform at various listening parties in Liège (6 November), Gent (7 November), Brussels (8 November), and Montreuil (4 December).

The post SHAPE+ Platform affiliate Pizza Noise Mafia set to release new album appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: The Underground announces full line-up for its AW25 season
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Running from 26 September - 31 December, Bristol’s The Underground completes its 2025 season with Avalon Emerson, Sherelle, HAAi, and more.

The post The Underground announces full line-up for its AW25 season appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Running from 26 September – 31 December, Bristol’s The Underground completes its 2025 season with Avalon Emerson, Sherelle, HAAi, and more.

Once a loading bay for a shopping centre in a former life, Bristol’s The Underground has been running events since 2021. Its stripped-back, bunker aesthetic and stellar sound system allow for a no-nonsense approach to clubbing. For its Autumn/Winter 2025 season, The Underground has pulled together a line-up of heavy hitters and emerging talent for a packed out schedule to see you through to NYE. 

The venue will host a strong selection of leading collectives, including Teletech, D&B All Stars, Elektrikal, and The Blast, for a programme that spans techno, house, grime, jungle, bass, garage and more.

Highlights include a night headlined by UK grime artist Ghetts with Sir Spyro & Footsie, Sicaria, Saint Ludo, and Oneman on 27 September. Halloween sees a live performance from 4AM Kru and sets from Sherelle b2b Naina, Samurai Breaks b2b Napes, and Pete Cannon b2b Silva Snipa.  

Other highlights include former Crack Magazine cover star Avalon Emerson, HAAi, Roi Perez, Ellie Stokes, DJ Boring, 1-800 Girls, Tarza, and Big Reg on 1 November. Mandidextrous will present Spectrum 360 Bristol on 6 December, while DNB All Stars take the NYE spot with a line-up yet to be announced. 

To view the full programme and purchase tickets, head to The Underground’s website.

 

The post The Underground announces full line-up for its AW25 season appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Rex Club announces new book and Ivan Smagghe-curated weekender with Craig Richards, Tiga, and more
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
The series of events will take place from 9-12 October at Rex Club in Paris, while the book, titled Nul si découvert, is set for release on 8 October.

The post Rex Club announces new book and Ivan Smagghe-curated weekender with Craig Richards, Tiga, and more appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The series of events will take place from 9-12 October at Rex Club in Paris, while the book, titled Nul si découvert, is set for release on 8 October.

In October, French DJ and producer Ivan Smagghe returns to Rex Club for three curated events. Having developed a relationship with the venue over the years, with past performances and appearing for the club’s 35th anniversary in 2023, Smagghe has brought together a line-up of past collaborators and iconic DJs. Set to perform over the three days are Craig Richards b2b Nicolas Lutz, Tiga, The Hacker, Chloé b2b Ivan Smagghe, Adi, Zadig, and Ivan Smagghe and Manfredas’ collaborative project, Dresden. 

Also set for release in October, Nul si découvert is a book “directed” by Ivan Smagghe and produced by Rex Club and Classic Paris. Nul si Découvert is described as a “book of perspectives – a book that lives between the lines” and explores Smagghe’s extensive relationship with Rex Club with contributions from Trevor Jackson, Philippe Azoury, Shanti Celeste, Gilbert Cohen, Craig Richards, Kate Green and more.

Rex Club’s programme for the remainder of 2025 welcomes a range of club nights with prominent DJs and promoters. Highlights include an all-nighter with Romanian DJ Priku (3 October), Antal and Flabaire Tatie Dee (4 October), Florian Picasso (16 October), Bass Culture with Raresh and D’Julz (18 October), and 30 years of legends with Moodymann, St Germain, Alex From Tokyo, and DJ Deep (9 November).

Visit Shotgun.live for more information on tickets.

The post Rex Club announces new book and Ivan Smagghe-curated weekender with Craig Richards, Tiga, and more appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Full of Lava announces full programme for 2025
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival will take place in Bern, Switzerland, from 20-23 November with Erika de Casier, John Glacier, Smerz, and more on the line-up.

The post Full of Lava announces full programme for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival will take place in Bern, Switzerland, from 20-23 November with Erika de Casier, John Glacier, Smerz, and more on the line-up.

Former power station, now cultural centre Dampfzentrale Bern, becomes the home of Full of Lava festival this winter, running from 20-23 November. Starting in 2008 as Saint Ghetto and changing its name to Full of Lava in 2023, the festival showcases leftfield and experimental sub-genres of popular music, presenting pop music as a sound that can be thought-provoking, challenge convention, and break away from tradition. 

The festival’s line-up brings together artists on the outer edges of pop music. Highlights include Norwegian electronic pop duo Smerz, saxophonist and electroacoustic musician Tapiwa Svosve, and Danish singer, songwriter, and producer Erika de Casier. Margaret Chardiet will bring her solo experimental project, Pharmakon, while Canadian musician Marie Davidson, American organist and composer Kali Malone, and English experimental group These New Puritans join the line-up. 

Other highlights include former Crack Magazine cover star John Glacier, eight-piece English collective, caroline, and a variety of late-night DJ sets from New York, i-vye, DJ E aka Chuquimamani-Condori, and Haya33.

More information and festival passes are available via the Dampfzentrale Bern website.

The post Full of Lava announces full programme for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: ROBOT Festival announces full programme for 2025
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
ROBOT Festival has revealed the full line-up for its 16th edition, which will take place from 9-11 October in Bologna, Italy.

The post ROBOT Festival announces full programme for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
ROBOT Festival has revealed the full line-up for its 16th edition, which will take place from 9-11 October in Bologna.

Exploring the theme of ‘Dream On’, Italy’s ROBOT Festival returns to Bologna this autumn, bringing a range of artists and sounds to the city’s industrial spaces, baroque halls, and ancient alleyways. 

The festival has now finalised the line-up, with Seefeel, Lucy Railton, Crystallmess, Safety Trance, Félicia Atkinson, and Tayhana. These new additions join already announced artists including Rainy Miller, Alessandro Cortini, DJ Haram, Apparat, Lorenzo Senni, Sama’ Abdulhadi, and Acid Arab. 

The main festival takes place from 9-11 October, with a special preview on 27 September in the historic Palazzo Re Enzo.

Tickets for individual shows and festival passes are available via the ROBOT website.

The post ROBOT Festival announces full programme for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Nick León, YHWH Nailgun and more join the line-up for Mucho Flow 2025
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Mucho Flow will return to various venues across the Portuguese city of Guimarães from 30 October - 1 November.

The post Nick León, YHWH Nailgun and more join the line-up for Mucho Flow 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
This autumn, avant-garde festival Mucho Flow returns to various venues across the Portuguese city of Guimarães for three days of experimental electronics and performance art.

Set to take place between 30 October – 1 November, the festival has announced new additions to the line-up, including Miami producer and DJ Nick León, experimental ambient composer Colombian Drone Mafia, and New York group YHWH Nailgun.

Other highlights include performances by bbb hairdryer, LUXE, Hannah Frances, Coletivo Raso, Body Meat, Noia, and Bolivian-American sonics from Los Thuthanaka.

These additions are set to join already confirmed artists, including Maria Somerville, These New Puritans, Lauren Duffus, Sassy 009, and Simo Cell b2b Verraco. 

Tickets are available now via the Mucho Flow website.

The post Nick León, YHWH Nailgun and more join the line-up for Mucho Flow 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: SHAPE+ Platform affiliate Farzané to perform at Rewire x Korzo
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
The Rewire x Korzo concert series returns on Friday, 3 October at The Hague’s Korzo Theatre.

The post SHAPE+ Platform affiliate Farzané to perform at Rewire x Korzo appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Rewire x Korzo concert series returns on Friday, 3 October at The Hague’s Korzo Theatre.

Launched in 2016, the series is presented in collaboration with Korzo Theatre and resumes this year after a two-year pause.

Among the highlights is Netherlands-based Iranian sound artist, musician, researcher and SHAPE+ affiliate Farzaneh Nouri, known artistically as Farzané. Working at the intersection of experimental music and computer science, her practice spans electroacoustic composition, creative coding, and linguistics. Following a spellbinding appearance at Rewire 2025, she will bring a new performance featuring self-developed sensored jewellery that directs two improvising AI agents live on stage.

Also on the bill is Brìghde Chaimbeul, the celebrated Scottish smallpipes and Great Highland bagpipe player whose sound is deeply rooted in Gaelic tradition, and Kara-Lis Coverdale, a Canadian composer and pianist whose emotionally charged works weave spectral textures and richly coloured harmonics.

For tickets and more details on upcoming events, visit the Rewire website.

The post SHAPE+ Platform affiliate Farzané to perform at Rewire x Korzo appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: En Masse announces the full line-up for its 2025 edition
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
DJ Python, Shackleton, Abdullah Miniawy and more are set to play the fifth edition of En Masse, which will take place from 9-12 October across multiple venues in Bristol.

The post En Masse announces the full line-up for its 2025 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
DJ Python, Shackleton, Abdullah Miniawy and more are set to play this year’s edition of En Masse, running from 9–12 October across multiple venues in Bristol.

Batu’s En Masse returns to Bristol this autumn, bringing together local talent and international artists for four days of experimentation, collaboration, and collective discovery.

For the festival’s fifth edition, the line-up includes singular live experiences and forward-thinking collaborations, while introducing a new Community Hub and an expanded programme with an extra day.

The music programme begins on 9 October with an opening night at Document, featuring patient, sculptural soundscapes from Mary Hurrell, an immersive hybrid performance from DJ Python, the Bristol debut of Abdullah Miniawy, and Coby Sey & Continue’s expansive live set.

The remainder of the festival moves fluidly between intimate spaces like The Island, The Love Inn, Strange Brew, and Trinity Centre. Highlights include sets from Amber b2b Laura Mackie, Farrell b2b Mackenzie, and Saskia b2b Jurango, as well as a debut collaboration between Batu and Dominica-born vocalist and producer Tikiman. Live performances will also come from Kakuhan, Loto Retina, Shackleton, and Twelfth House.

Funded by Arts Council England, the Community & Arts Programme opens on 7 October with free workshops, talks, and participatory sessions. Highlights include a technical workshop with Shackleton at Elevator Sound, an audio-visual installation by Avon Terror Corps’ Dali de Saint Paul & Clíona Ní Laoi at St John’s Crypt, and a special discussion with ex.sses and Õkami (Sisu Crew) exploring how dance music communities can mobilise in solidarity with Palestine.

Festival Passes are on sale now at the festival’s website and via Headfirst, granting access to core programming from 9–12 October (Thursday–Saturday). Passes are also the only way to access the limited-capacity Saturday After Party. Individual tickets are also available here.

The post En Masse announces the full line-up for its 2025 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Skaņu Mežs announces more artists and commissioned works for 2025
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
The experimental music festival returns to Riga, Latvia, from 3-4 October.

The post Skaņu Mežs announces more artists and commissioned works for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The experimental music festival returns to Riga, Latvia, from 3-4 October.

Skaņu Mežs will take over Hanzas Perons, a warehouse reborn as a concert hall with over a century of history, for its 23rd edition next month.

The festival has now revealed its next wave of artists, including 2024/2025 SHAPE+ Platform artist R3YWYA, a noise-based project by Evija Ābrama.

Across the two days, Skaņu Mežs will host performances by multi-disciplinary artist and composer Laura Ortman, Glasgow-based DJ and experimental artist KAVARI, and a collaborative performance featuring London-based saxophonist John Butcher and Philadelphia-based artist and musician Bill Nace. 

Other highlights include Latvian composer and sound installation artist Ernests Vilsons, who will present the world premiere of his piece all else erased, performed by double bassist Laine Luīze Freidenberga. 

A commissioned piece of audio-visual work from artists Pierce Warnecke & Matthew Biederman will be shown at the festival. Titled Phytomorphic Topographies, the project explores the delicate ecosystems of Latvia’s wilderness through immersive 3D visuals and near-field sound recordings. 

Visit the Skaņu Mežs website for tickets, with further acts, workshops, and installations to be announced soon.

The post Skaņu Mežs announces more artists and commissioned works for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Le Guess Who? announces full programme for 2025
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival will take place in Utrecht from 6–9 November with Smerz, Devendra Banhart, Ata Kak and more on the line-up.

The post Le Guess Who? announces full programme for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival will take place in Utrecht from 6–9 November with Smerz, Devendra Banhart, Ata Kak and more on the line-up.

Le Guess Who? returns to the city of Utrecht in the Netherlands this November, with a programme that once again exists to gather groundbreaking experimental artists and platform fresh perspectives on what’s possible in music.

Today (3 September), the festival has announced new additions to the line-up.

New names include Norwegian electronic pop duo Smerz, renowned Cape Verdean artist Maria Alice, and composer and vocalist Hania Rani, who will introduce her alter ego Chilling Bambino. Legendary Ghanaian musician Ata Kak will perform his first new album in over 30 years; Italian musician Daniela Pes and composer-producer IOSONOUNCANE will deliver a collaborative original set; and Tunisian-Dutch composer and performer Skander Jaïbi will present his debut album Who Has the Right to Closure? alongside choreographer-dancer Alicia Verdú Macián, Utrecht’s Flare Quartet and the Choir Lex et Gaudium.

Returning artists include 2018 guest curator Devendra Banhart, who will give a career-spanning solo show, and Sudanese multidisciplinary artist MAHA.

Le Guess Who? has also announced a club night on 7 November, with Brazilian collective BATEKOO teaming up with Amsterdam’s Los Angles to celebrate queer communities through performances from Linn da Quebrada, Mina Galán and more. The festival also revealed its daily “secret performance” series, inviting audiences to embrace the unknown and catch surprise artists on its main stages.

Previously announced performers include The Congos, Rizwan-Muazzam Qawwali, SUNN O))), Unwound, Saul Williams & Carlos Niño, and Lido Pimienta. For this year’s edition, the festival’s guest curators include Amirtha Kidambi, Asher Gamedze, Edna Martinez, gyrofield, Lonnie Holley, Tianzhuo Chen, Valentina Magaletti and Ziúr.

Remaining day passes are available now at Le Guess Who?’s website.

The post Le Guess Who? announces full programme for 2025 appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: The Warehouse Project announces additional shows for WHP25 season
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The Warehouse Project adds three new shows to this year's programme, including the return of Nia Archives' club series Up Ya Archives.

The post The Warehouse Project announces additional shows for WHP25 season appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Manchester promoter has revealed three more shows for its WHP25 season, running from September to NYE.

This week, The Warehouse Project added three shows to its current programme, including the return of club series Up Ya Archives from DJ and producer Nia Archives. 

Berlioz will bring his Deep In It concept to Depot Mayfield for a headline show on Saturday, 8 November. The night will feature Berlioz alongside Chaos in the CBD, Cody Currie, and Jeremy Underground.  

Set for Saturday, 15 November, Nia Archives’ Up Ya Archives clubs series will return for its biggest addition yet. Headlined by Nia herself, the night will feature sets from Skream & Benga, Girls Don’t Sync, DJ Storm b2b DJ Flight b2b Mantra, Mala, Sam Binga, Bambii, Sexy Lady Massive, Sir Spyro b2b Maximum, and a DJ set from Fcukers. 

As part of Up Ya Archives, the Concourse Stage will host a fifth anniversary party for record label ec2a. Presented by Dr Dubplate, the stage will feature sets from former Crack Magazine cover star Anz, Bakey b2b Mia Koden, Yemz, and Skeptic.

On Saturday, 29 November, The Warehouse Project’s flagship techno showcase XXL will return to close out the month. This year’s line-up features sets from 999999999, Azyr, Fantasm, Kander, Partiboi69, Adrian Mills b2b Cloudy, BYØRN, and Fumi.

Other parties across the autumn and winter schedule include Overmono, Confidence Man, Black Coffee, and DJ Heartstring. 

Sign up for pre-sale tickets and view all other WHP25 events through The Warehouse Project website. 

Pre-sale tickets go live at 9am on Friday, 29 August, and general tickets go live at 12pm on Friday, 29 August.

The post The Warehouse Project announces additional shows for WHP25 season appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Llais Festival announces new artists for its 2025 edition
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The festival returns to Wales Millennium Centre from 6-12 October as part of Cardiff Music City Festival.

The post Llais Festival announces new artists for its 2025 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The festival returns to Wales Millennium Centre from 6-12 October as part of Cardiff Music City Festival.

Set for its sixth edition this October, Llais brings together global voices for a unique indoor festival experience at Cardiff’s Wales Millennium Centre. 

The festival will take place as part of Cardiff Music City Festival: a two-week-long celebration of music, talks, installations, performances, and technology. 

The latest announcement sees Meredith Monk make her long-awaited Welsh debut, performing in Hoddinott Hall alongside longtime collaborators Katie Geissinger and Allison Sniffin. Also joining the line-up is folk and indie-pop artist Mared, performing with her seven-piece band, and Khamira, a collective that fuses Welsh folk and jazz with Hindustani classical music. Meanwhile, Poesis – featuring Welsh-born Claire Victoria Roberts, Lulu Manning (Scotland), Conor McAuley (Northern Ireland), and Xhosa Cole (England) – unites voices from across the UK for a fresh take on traditional folk.

These acts join previously announced artists Rufus Wainwright, Cate Le Bon, Annie and the Caldwells, Mabe Fratti, Enji and Beverly Glenn-Copeland with special guest Elizabeth Copeland. Vieux Farka Touré and Trio Da Kali are also on the line-up, as well as Ibibio Sound Machine and RÓIS. 

Visit the Wales Millennium Centre website for more information and tickets.

The post Llais Festival announces new artists for its 2025 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Beyond The Valley announces 10th anniversary line-up
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Addison Rae, Turnstile, 070 Shake and more will play the Australian festival in December.

The post Beyond The Valley announces 10th anniversary line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Addison Rae, Turnstile, 070 Shake and more will play the Australian festival in December.

Australia’s Beyond The Valley (BTV) will mark its 10th anniversary this year with a four-day celebration of music and art. Taking place from 28 December to 1 January at Barunah Plains, Wadawurrung Country, Victoria, the festival’s programme spans pop, indie, rap, hip-hop, electronic music and more.

Headliners include Addison Rae, Kid Cudi and Turnstile, with Crack Magazine cover star 070 Shake, NYC dance-pop duo Fcukers, Compton-raised all-rounder Channel Tres, techno DJ VTSS and Chicago-based musician Jane Remover also on the bill.

Dozens of DJs have also been announced, including a range of both local talent and global names.

Over the past decade, Beyond The Valley has hosted artists including Tyler, The Creator, and has grown to become Australia’s largest camping festival.

“Ten years ago, we could never have imagined what BTV would become,” says Nicholas Greco, Co-Founder & Managing Partner of Untitled Group. “It’s now a rite of passage for so many, and this anniversary is our most ambitious celebration yet.”

This year’s edition will see the festival’s three main stages return bigger and better, with the Valley Stage reimagined alongside UK design studio Vision Factory and a newly revamped Dance Dome.

More artists, as well as live activations, comedy, podcasts and art installations will be announced over the coming months.

Presale begins on Thursday, 28 August at 9am BST/ 10am CET. General sale begins on Friday, 29 August. Visit Beyond The Valley’s website for the full line-up and tickets.

The post Beyond The Valley announces 10th anniversary line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: AlphaTheta expands availability of Chordcat Groovebox worldwide
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The portable 8-track instrument features intuitive chord creation and sound design tools.

The post AlphaTheta expands availability of Chordcat Groovebox worldwide appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The portable 8-track instrument features intuitive chord creation and sound design tools.

AlphaTheta has announced the global rollout of Chordcat, its compact 8-track groovebox designed to help creators generate chord progressions, build full arrangements, and capture ideas on the go.

Central to the device is Chord Cruiser mode, which draws from a library of more than 110,000 chord combinations to suggest compatible transitions in real time. Users can also access 18 preset progressions, covering genres from house to future bass, as well as essential major and minor templates.

Chordcat’s production features include a 16-step sequencer with 128 steps (8 bars), support for up to 8 instrument tracks, 145 onboard sounds, 16 drum kits, and three XY-pad-controlled effects (arpeggiator, delay, and ducker). The Running Direction function adds further flexibility by allowing patterns to be rearranged in multiple playback directions.

The post AlphaTheta expands availability of Chordcat Groovebox worldwide appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Axel Arigato introduces immersive new campaign, Footnotes
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
The campaign reimagines city life as a stage for music, movement and design.

The post Axel Arigato introduces immersive new campaign, Footnotes appeared first on Crack Magazine.
