item.title: Get On Festival set to return this September
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Mia Koden, Dr Banana, Lukas Wigflex and more are set to play the Exeter-based festival from 5-7 September.

The post Get On Festival set to return this September appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Mia Koden, Dr Banana, Lukas Wigflex, and more are set to play the Exeter-based festival from 5-7 September.

Get On Festival returns to Great Fulford for three days and nights this September. Located in a picturesque estate just outside of Exeter, the festival’s third edition promises a range of underground dance music, top-quality food vendors, a swimming lake, and a designated wellness area.

With over 75 artists on the bill, the programme spans genres from drum & bass, garage, 140, dubstep, and more. 

Set to headline the festival are Fabio & GQ, Dr Banana, Tasha, Lukas Wigflex, Mia Koden, and Sir Hiss. Other highlights include DJ ADHD, Aaee, Ema, Jenny Sparks, Dual Monitor, BL3SS, Fonzo, Jurango, and Charles Green. 

Both main stages from previous editions will return: The Villa, based on a Sardinian villa balcony, and The Wine Cellar, inspired by the crew’s time spent at a friend’s West Country Vineyard. A stand-out from last year was the stage Le Grille, a giant barrel BBQ-shaped stage creating a haze of smoke while DJs perform throughout the day. 

Tickets for Get On Festival are almost sold out. Head over to the website for the full line-up, more information, and to purchase tickets.

The post Get On Festival set to return this September appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Biennale Musica releases full programme and tickets for its 2025 edition
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Biennale Musica has revealed its full programme and opened ticket sales for its 69th edition. Running from 11-25 October in various venues across Venice, the festival will be directed by Italian composer and musician Caterina Barbieri. 

The post Biennale Musica releases full programme and tickets for its 2025 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
The Italian contemporary music and arts festival returns to Venice from 11-25 October.

Biennale Musica has revealed its full programme and opened ticket sales for its 69th edition. Running from 11-25 October in various venues across Venice, the festival will be directed by Italian composer and musician Caterina Barbieri. 

The festival’s line-up pulls together a variety of experimental boundary-pushing artists. Highlights include Sunn O))), Laurie Spiegel, William Basinski, Carl Craig, Suzanne Ciani with Actress, plus Silver Lion awardee Chuquimamani-Condori. 

Other names include Mia Koden, Ecco2k, DJ Marcelle, Moritz Von Oswold, Bendik Giske, Yosuke Fujita, Kamigaku Ensemble, Catherine Christer Hennix, and STILL (Simone Trabucchi). 

At the LSD Centre (La Stella Dentro Centre), a venue for deep listening sessions, live performances, and lectures, two large-scale installations will take place throughout the festival: Maxime Denuc’s Elevations and the winner of the Golden Lion Lifetime Achievement Award and Meredith Monk’s Songs of Ascension Shrine.

Taking place on 19 October, a special event titled Star Chamber – Secret Island will transport audiences from Riva degli Schiavoni via boat towards an island of the Venetian lagoon, for a site-specific performance exploring sound, space, and perception.

Under the theme The Star Within, Barbieri frames the festival as a journey into cosmic music, exploring generative music, music as a living organism, and as an autopoietic form able to self-evolve. 

 “The desire for great things and vastness. A vibration that permeates the cosmos and pierces through us with awe, from molecule to planetary motion, sound escapes the boundaries of the ego and opens us up to the encounter with the Other – the unknown.” Says Barbieri. 

Various tickets and subscriptions for the festival are available through Biennale Musica’s website.

The post Biennale Musica releases full programme and tickets for its 2025 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: WOS Festival announces full line-up for its 2025 edition
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Taking place from 11-14 September in Spain’s Santiago de Compostela, KAVARI, Purelink, Niecy Blues, and more join the line-up for WOS Festival.

The post WOS Festival announces full line-up for its 2025 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Taking place from 11-14 September in Spain’s Santiago de Compostela, KAVARI, Purelink, Rainy Miller, and more join the line-up for WOS Festival.

Held in collaboration with SON Estrella Galicia, WOS Festival returns to the north-western Spanish city of Santiago de Compostela next month, bringing an eclectic line-up to ten venues across the picturesque Galician capital. The festival, in its eighth year, will spill out across churches, clubs and unconventional spaces, bringing a carefully curated, multidisciplinary programme.

WOS Festival has added fifteen names to its line-up, among these are KAVARI, Sara Persico & Mike Oki, Niecy Blues, Klein, former Crack Magazine cover star Purelink, and Rainy Miller presents Joseph, What Have You Done? 

Other highlights include Carrier presents Rhythm Immortal, KUNTARI, BFlecha b2b Mwëslee, and Saint Abdullah, Eomac & Rebecca Salvadori present A Forbidden Distance.

These additions join previously announced artists Batu, CCL, Beatrice Dillon, Djrum, Objekt, and Aya & MFO. 

Purchase tickets and view the full line-up via the WOS Festival website.

The post WOS Festival announces full line-up for its 2025 edition appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: What’s happening at We Out Here Festival this weekend
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
We Out Here returns to Wimborne St Giles in Dorset from 14-17 August, with Nala Sinephro, Batu, Kokoroko, and Loyle Carner on the bill.

The post What’s happening at We Out Here Festival this weekend appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
We Out Here returns to Wimborne St Giles in Dorset this weekend, with Nala Sinephro, Batu, Kokoroko, and Loyle Carner on the bill.

Running from 14-17 August, Gilles Peterson’s We Out Here Festival is set for its sixth edition with a roster of over 100 artists ready to perform in the idyllic Dorset countryside. 

The festival’s programme begins today (Thursday 14 August), with a daytime line-up of Tina Edwards, SHOLTO, and an eight-hour set from Theo Parrish. Into the evening, Dar Disku, former Crack Magazine cover star Shy One, and Nubiyan Twist are set to perform, as well as Ikonika, Tim Reaper, Sully, Dwarde, and this month’s Crack Magazine cover star Noname.

Friday’s daytime schedule sees Gilles Peterson kick things off, followed by Channel One, Ruby Savage, and Astrid Sonne. The evening begins with a performance from Nala Sinephro, as well as Pa Salieu, James Mason, Mia Koden, and former Crack Magazine: The Collections cover star Batu. The evening will close out with a solo set from Josey Rebelle, followed by a b2b with Ben UFO. 

Saturday’s daytime highlights include Marysia Osu, Smith & Mighty featuring Tammy Payne, Bukky Leo, Lex Amor, and Rotary Connection 22. The evening programme opens with sets from Luke Una, Bashy, Hunee, Neffa-T, and Azu Tiwaline, followed by Al Wootton, K Means & i-sha, Peverelist & Yushh, Om Unit, CCL, Roni Size, and Objekt. 

The final day of the festival welcomes Kahil El’Zabar, Aba-Shanti-I, Ganavya, Emma-Jean Thackray, and Kaitlyn Aurelia Smith in the daytime. While the evening highlights include Jimi Tenor, Kokoroko, Jamz Supernova, Addison Groove & Sister Zo, and Loyle Carner, stepping in for Michael Kiwanuka, closing out the main stage. The festival’s remaining closing sets come courtesy of Gilles Peterson, Sam Binga & Fracture, and 4AM KRU. 

Alongside the music programme, We Out Here will host a range of talks in the Talks Tent, from artist interviews to spoken word and immersive performances. 

We Out Here is now sold out, but check out the full line-up and set times on the festival’s website. 

 

The post What’s happening at We Out Here Festival this weekend appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Spatial Festival 2025 heads to Berlin’s Funkhaus this September
item.dc:creator: Crack Staff
item.description:
Loraine James, Space Afrika, Richie Culver, Gaika and more pencilled in for three-day pioneering art...

The post Spatial Festival 2025 heads to Berlin’s Funkhaus this September appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Loraine James, Space Afrika, Richie Culver, Gaika and more pencilled in for three-day pioneering art and music event.

Audiophile paradise, Spatial Festival, returns to Berlin next month (September 12-14) for a three day exploration of art and music, taking over the city’s iconic Funkhaus. This year the festival will spill out across the many chambers and rooms of the Funkhaus, unleashing 60 pioneering artists across a programme of live performances, listening sessions and art installations. 

The Funkhaus boasts some of the world’s most acoustically refined spaces, from recording halls to monolithic resonance studios. This year’s festival will build on the venue’s remarkable audio heritage with the help of the 4DSOUND system, a holographic sound instrument developed by spatial design studio, MONOM – the result of a decade of artistic and technical research. Rooms will be turned into sonic instruments, aimed at creating the purest sound possible.

Honouring these remarkable acoustic surroundings, the festival will see artists presenting original work across audio and visual. Loraine James, Abyss X and others will present listening sessions, alongside live performances from Gaika, Richie Culver, Evita Manji and more. This year, the festival is also teaming up with Berlin Art Week and curatorial platform PASSAGE for the first time to deliver an ambitious art programme. Curated by Victor Auberjonois and Konrad Biedenkopf, the programme will feature works from a host of international artists including Manchester multidisciplinarians Space Afrika. 

For full line-up and ticket information head to the Spatial Festival website. 

The post Spatial Festival 2025 heads to Berlin’s Funkhaus this September appeared first on Crack Magazine.

item.title: Lunchmeat Festival announces new additions to 2025 line-up
item.dc:creator: Tom Pittard
item.description:
Rainy Miller, estoc, Kodiki, and more join the programme for the Prague-based festival from 22-28 September.

The post Lunchmeat Festival announces new additions to 2025 line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
item.content:encoded:
Slikback, Rainy Miller, estoc, Kodiki, among second wave announcements for the Prague-based festival.

Taking place across the week of 22-28 September, Lunchmeat Festival is set to take over four venues across the Czech capital for its 16th edition. Phase two of the festival line-up builds on its existing connections with record label SVBKVLT and contemporary arts festival MUTEK Montreal, showcasing exciting names in ambient and electronic music amongst some of the city’s most striking architecture. 

Two world premieres top the recent announcement. SVBKVLT’s estoc will bring a live audiovisual performance How Can You Possibly Bear To Think About Anything Else?, whilst experimental producer Slikback presents a collaborative show with visual artists Malt Disney and Lunchmeat’s own Tasya. The trio will perform improvised themes from his latest album Attrition. 

In collaboration with MUTEK Montreal, audiovisual artist Baron Lanteigne adapts his hard data / soft bodies AV installation for Prague’s CAMP venue (complete with unique 24x4m projection surface), while Mathieu Arsenault AKA Seulement presents his show Bricolage Architecture. The second wave announcement also includes AV shows from Malibu, Palmistry, Kodiki, Violent Magic Orchestra, and a live show from Aho Ssan & Resina performing as Ego Death. Capping things off, Rainy Miller brings a live AV performance of recent album Joseph, What Have You Done? 

These additions join already announced artists Gorgonn & Utku Önal, Danny L Harle, Bristol-based producer and visual artist Mun Sing, and UIQ label head Lee Gamble.

More names are set to be announced soon, including Lunchmeat Festival’s symposium.

Visit the festival’s website for tickets and more information.

The post Lunchmeat Festival announces new additions to 2025 line-up appeared first on Crack Magazine.
